AICompletionResult.description: The result of an AI chat completion request. Returned by chat completion endpoints after the model finishes generating.

message.content: How can I help you today?

messages.content: How can I help you today?

finish_reason.description: The reason the model stopped generating. Common values include `"stop"` (natural end), `"length"` (token limit reached), and `"tool_calls"` (the model invoked a tool).

message.description: The final assistant message produced by the completion.

messages.description: The complete message history for the conversation, including all user, assistant, and tool messages in order.

token_usage.description: Token consumption breakdown keyed by model ID. Each value is a map with `"input_tokens"` and `"output_tokens"` counts. `null` when usage data is unavailable.

AIImageResult.description: The result returned by an AI image generation or editing operation. Contains the generated image (as inline data or a URL) along with dimension, size, and usage metadata.

example.revised_prompt: A photorealistic image of a sunset over the ocean with warm golden hues.

aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio of the generated image, e.g. `"16:9"` or `"1:1"`. `null` when not reported by the provider.

height.description: Height of the generated image in pixels. `null` when the provider does not report dimensions.

image_data.description: The generated image encoded as a base64 string. Present when the provider returns inline image data. `null` when `image_url` is set instead.

image_size.description: Resolution tier label for the image, e.g. `"1K"` or `"2K"`. `null` when the provider does not include a tier label.

image_type.description: MIME type of the generated image, e.g. `"image/png"` or `"image/jpeg"`. `null` when the provider does not report a content type.

image_url.description: Temporary URL pointing to the generated image hosted by the provider. Present when the provider returns a URL rather than inline data. `null` when `image_data` is set instead.

model.description: Identifier of the model that produced the image, e.g. `"dall-e-3"` or `"imagen-3"`.

revised_prompt.description: The prompt as rewritten by the provider before generation. Some providers (e.g. DALL-E 3) automatically expand or safety-check the original prompt. `null` when the provider does not revise prompts.
revised_prompt.example: A photorealistic image of a sunset over the ocean with warm golden hues.

size.description: Canonical size string as returned by the provider, e.g. `"1024x1024"`. `null` when not reported.

usage.description: Provider-reported token and compute usage for the request. Structure varies by provider. `null` when usage data is unavailable.

width.description: Width of the generated image in pixels. `null` when the provider does not report dimensions.

AIMessage.description: A single message in an AI conversation, following the OpenAI-compatible chat format. Used in both request inputs and completion responses.

example.content: How can I help you today?

content.description: Plain-text content of the message. Present for `system`, `user`, and `assistant` messages. `null` when the message body is expressed through `content_parts` or `tool_calls`.
content.example: How can I help you today?

content_parts.description: Multimodal content parts for the message, used when the body includes images or mixed media. Each part is a map with a `type` key (`"text"`, `"image_url"`, or `"image_data"`). `null` when `content` is set.

resume_token.description: Opaque token that can be passed on a subsequent request to resume this conversation from the current state. `null` when the provider does not support conversation resumption.

role.description: The speaker role for this message. One of `"system"`, `"user"`, `"assistant"`, or `"tool"`.

structured_output.description: Parsed structured data returned by the model when a JSON schema or structured-output mode was requested. Shape varies by the schema supplied at call time. `null` when structured output was not requested.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls requested by the model in an `assistant` message. Present only on assistant messages that invoke one or more tools. `null` on all other message roles.

tool_results.description: Tool execution results provided in a `tool` message. Each entry corresponds to a prior tool call by its `id`. `null` on all other message roles.

AIToolCall.description: A tool (function) call emitted by the assistant within an AI message. Mirrors the OpenAI tool-call object format.

arguments.description: Arguments the model wants to pass to the tool, as a key-value map. Deserialize and validate these against the tool's input schema before executing.

id.description: Unique identifier for this tool call, assigned by the model. Use this value as `id` when submitting the corresponding tool result.

name.description: Name of the tool or function the model wants to invoke, e.g. `"web_search"` or `"run_code"`.

thought_signature.description: Opaque signature representing the model's internal reasoning that led to this tool call. `null` when the provider does not expose chain-of-thought data.

AIToolResult.description: The result of executing a tool call, submitted back to the model as a tool-role message. Mirrors the OpenAI tool-result object format.

example.content: string
example.name: Example Name

content.description: Plain-text output produced by the tool execution. `null` when the result is expressed entirely through `resolution`.

id.description: ID of the tool call this result satisfies. Must match the `id` from the corresponding `AIToolCall`.

name.description: Name of the tool or function that was executed, e.g. `"web_search"`. Must match the `name` from the corresponding `AIToolCall`.

resolution.description: Structured result data from the tool execution. Shape varies by tool. `null` when the result is expressed as plain text in `content`.

Acl.description: An access-control list payload that supports either full replacement or targeted patch operations on a resource's grants.

add.description: Patch mode: grants to add or merge into the existing list. Cannot be combined with `grants`.

grants.description: Replace mode: the complete new list of grants that replaces all existing entries. Send an empty array (`[]`) to clear all grants. Cannot be combined with `add` or `remove`.

remove.description: Patch mode: principals whose grants should be removed from the existing list. Cannot be combined with `grants`.

AclGrant.description: A single access-control grant that pairs a principal with the set of actions it is allowed to perform.

actions.description: Array of action strings the principal is permitted to perform, e.g. `["read", "write"]`. Must contain at least one entry.

principal.description: The identifier of the principal. A string ID for `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, and `"agent"` types; one of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"` for `"org_role"`; omit entirely when `principal_type` is `"everyone"`.

principal_type.description: The kind of principal receiving the grant. One of `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, `"org_role"`, `"agent"`, or `"everyone"`.

AclRemoveTarget.description: Identifies a principal to be removed from an access-control list.

principal.description: The identifier of the principal to remove. A string ID for `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, and `"agent"` types; one of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"` for `"org_role"`. Omit when `principal_type` is `"everyone"`.

principal_type.description: The kind of principal to remove. One of `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, `"org_role"`, `"agent"`, or `"everyone"`.

ActivityFeedEntry.description: A single event record in an activity feed, capturing what happened, who caused it, and which resources were involved.

example.content: The agent completed the task successfully.
example.title: Example Title

agent.description: The agent that produced this event. Returns an agent ID (`agi_...`) by default, or an expanded agent object when the association is loaded. `null` if no agent is associated.

oneOf.description: An AI agent that can be configured with tools, routines, and skills, and invoked to handle conversations or tasks.

example.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
example.name: Example Name

acl.description: Access control list for the agent. Contains a `grants` array where each entry specifies `principal_type`, `principal`, and `actions`. `null` when no ACL restrictions are applied and the agent is accessible to all members of its scope.

add.description: Patch mode: grants to add or merge into the existing list. Cannot be combined with `grants`.

items.description: A single access-control grant that pairs a principal with the set of actions it is allowed to perform.

actions.description: Array of action strings the principal is permitted to perform, e.g. `["read", "write"]`. Must contain at least one entry.

principal.description: The identifier of the principal. A string ID for `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, and `"agent"` types; one of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"` for `"org_role"`; omit entirely when `principal_type` is `"everyone"`.

principal_type.description: The kind of principal receiving the grant. One of `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, `"org_role"`, `"agent"`, or `"everyone"`.

grants.description: Replace mode: the complete new list of grants that replaces all existing entries. Send an empty array (`[]`) to clear all grants. Cannot be combined with `add` or `remove`.

items.description: A single access-control grant that pairs a principal with the set of actions it is allowed to perform.

actions.description: Array of action strings the principal is permitted to perform, e.g. `["read", "write"]`. Must contain at least one entry.

principal.description: The identifier of the principal. A string ID for `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, and `"agent"` types; one of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"` for `"org_role"`; omit entirely when `principal_type` is `"everyone"`.

principal_type.description: The kind of principal receiving the grant. One of `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, `"org_role"`, `"agent"`, or `"everyone"`.

remove.description: Patch mode: principals whose grants should be removed from the existing list. Cannot be combined with `grants`.

items.description: Identifies a principal to be removed from an access-control list.

principal.description: The identifier of the principal to remove. A string ID for `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, and `"agent"` types; one of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"` for `"org_role"`. Omit when `principal_type` is `"everyone"`.

principal_type.description: The kind of principal to remove. One of `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, `"org_role"`, `"agent"`, or `"everyone"`.

app.description: ID of the application that owns this agent (`dap_...`).

default_model.description: Default LLM model identifier used by this agent when no model is specified at runtime (e.g. `"claude-3-7-sonnet-latest"`).

email.description: Email address provisioned for this agent. `null` if email delivery is not configured.

identity.description: System-level identity prompt that shapes the agent's persona and behavior.
identity.example: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.

last_applied_template_config.description: ID of the AgentTemplate config (`cfg_...`) this agent was last provisioned or updated from. `null` for manually created agents.

lookup_key.description: Stable, user-defined identifier for this agent within the application. Unique per app.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the agent. Not interpreted by the platform.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the agent. `null` if not set.

org.description: ID of the organization this agent belongs to (`org_...`). `null` if the agent is not org-scoped.

org_name.description: Display name of the organization this agent belongs to. `null` when the agent is not org-scoped or when the org association was not preloaded.

originator.description: Free-form label identifying the source or author that created this agent (e.g. a username or pipeline name).

phone_number.description: Phone number provisioned for this agent. `null` if SMS is not configured.

sandbox.description: ID of the sandbox environment this agent is scoped to (`dsb_...`). `null` in production deployments.

source_solution.description: Source Solution and AgentTemplate summary for agents provisioned from a Solution. Includes `upgrade_available`, `latest_version`, and `latest_solution` so you can render an upgrade badge without a separate dry-run call. `null` for hand-built agents and agents whose tracked template or parent Solution has been deleted. Populated only on single-agent GET responses, never on list endpoints.

solution.description: Summary of the parent Solution, including `upgrade_available`, `latest_version`, and `latest_solution` when a newer system-scoped version is available for the agent's org-scoped Solution.

category_keys.description: Category tag keys declared in the Solution body, used to group Solutions in the catalog. An empty array when the body declares none.

created_at.description: When the Solution config was first imported (ISO 8601).

description.description: Short tagline or summary declared in the Solution body, used as the card subhead in catalog UIs. `null` when the Solution body does not set one.

kind.description: Resource type. Always `"Solution"`.

latest_solution.description: When `upgrade_available` is `true`, the system-scope Solution config ID (`cfg_...`) that should be used as the upgrade source. `null` otherwise.

latest_version.description: When `upgrade_available` is `true`, the higher system-scope `solution_version` available to upgrade to. `null` otherwise.

lookup_key.description: The lookup key stored on the Solution config, if one was assigned during import. `null` when no lookup key was set.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata declared in the Solution body (e.g. category or display hints). Present as an empty object when the body declares none.

name.description: Human-facing display name declared in the Solution body. `null` when the Solution body does not set one.

org.description: Organization ID (`org_...`) that owns this Solution config, when the Solution is scoped to a specific org. `null` for system-scope (app-level) Solutions.

org_logo.description: Canonical image-source object for the resolved `org`'s logo, used as the principal category section glyph. Carries the signed `url` plus a `refresh_url`. `null` when `org_slug` is `null` or the org has no logo.

file.description: ID of the underlying storage file (`fil_...`). `null` when the image is not backed by a platform storage file.

height.description: Height of the image in pixels. `null` if not known.

media.description: ID of the associated media record (`med_...`). `null` when the image is not linked to a media entity.

mime_type.description: MIME type of the image, e.g. `"image/png"` or `"image/jpeg"`. `null` if not known.

refresh_url.description: Endpoint URL you can call to obtain a fresh signed `url` when the current one has expired. `null` if the URL does not require refreshing.

url.description: Signed or public URL for downloading the image. May be time-limited; use `refresh_url` to obtain a new URL when this one expires.

width.description: Width of the image in pixels. `null` if not known.

org_name.description: Display name of the resolved `org`. Pairs with `org_slug` as the principal catalog category's label. `null` when `org_slug` is `null`.

org_slug.description: Resolved slug of the Solution body's `org` (the publishing organization), when set and it resolves to a real org visible to the viewer. When present this is the Solution's principal catalog category key — clients group the Solution under this org ahead of `category_keys`. `null` when the body has no `org` or it doesn't resolve.

owners.description: Owner scopes this Solution appears under. Members: `"system"` (app-level system scope) and/or `"org"` (viewer's org scope).

readme_url.description: Relative path to the public README endpoint with a signed token already embedded. `null` when the Solution has no README. Token expires in 1 hour — refresh via `GET /api/v1/solutions/:solution`.

solution_id.description: Stable UUID declared in the Solution body, used to identify the same logical Solution across multiple installed copies and owner scopes. `null` when the body omits it.

solution_version.description: Semver string declared in the Solution body (e.g. `"1.2.0"`). `null` when the body does not declare a version.

tag_keys.description: Freeform tag keys declared in the Solution body. An empty array when the body declares none.

template_kind.description: Wrapped template kind — `"AgentTemplate"`, `"AutomationTemplate"`, `"AgentRoutineTemplate"`, `"AgentToolTemplate"`, `"AgentComputerTemplate"`, or `"SolutionTemplateRef"` for ref-mode bundles.

templates.description: Template configs bundled by this Solution, in declaration order — the first entry is the deployable template the Solution wraps; the rest are sibling templates the wrapped template references.

items.description: Identity and display metadata for a single template bundled by a Solution, used to represent each wrapped or sibling template at template granularity.

description.description: Short prose blurb from the template body's `description:` field. `null` when the body doesn't set one. Used as the card subhead in the Library carousel.

display_name.description: Human-facing label from the template body's `display_name:` field. `null` when the body doesn't set one. Library carousels use this for the card title, falling back to a humanized `name`.

id.description: Template config ID (`cfg_...`). `null` for inline-only templates.

kind.description: Template config kind, or `SolutionTemplateRef` / `SolutionTemplatePath` when unresolved.

lookup_key.description: Lookup key stamped on the template config at import time. `null` when no lookup key was assigned.

name.description: Canonical name from the template body. For `AgentTemplate` this doubles as the human-facing label; for `AgentToolTemplate` it's the LLM-facing tool function identifier (snake_case); for `AgentRoutineTemplate` it's the routine identifier (kebab-case). Clients rendering carousels should prefer `display_name` and fall back to humanizing `name`.

readme_url.description: Relative path to the public README endpoint with a signed token already embedded, scoped to this template's bundled markdown asset. `null` when the Solution body's `templates[].readme_path` is unset for this entry. Token expires in 1 hour — refresh via `GET /api/v1/solutions/:solution`.

virtual_path.description: Stable virtual path assigned to the template config. `null` when no virtual path was set.

updated_at.description: When the Solution config was last modified (ISO 8601).

upgrade_available.description: `true` when this Solution is installed at the viewer's org scope and the app-level system scope carries a higher `solution_version`. Always `false` for system-only rows.

virtual_path.description: The stable virtual path assigned to this Solution config, used as the deduplication key when the same Solution appears under multiple owner scopes. `null` when unset.

template.description: Summary of the AgentTemplate config (`cfg_...`) the agent was last provisioned or updated from.

created_at.description: When this template config was created (ISO 8601).

description.description: Description of the template from the config body. `null` if the current version has no `description` field.

display_name.description: Human-readable display name from the config body. `null` if the current version has no `display_name` field.

kind.description: Config kind identifier for this template (e.g. `"agent_tool_template"`).

lookup_key.description: Stable lookup key assigned to this template config. `null` if no lookup key is set.

name.description: Template name as stored in the config body. `null` if the current version has no `name` field.

updated_at.description: When this template config was last modified (ISO 8601).

virtual_path.description: Virtual filesystem path for this template config. `null` if not set.

team.description: ID of the team that owns this agent (`tem_...`). `null` if the agent is not team-scoped.

updated_at.description: When the agent was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user that owns this agent (`usr_...`). `null` if the agent is not user-scoped.

app.description: ID of the application that produced this entry (`dap_...`). `null` if not scoped to an app.

attachments.description: Array of attachment objects associated with this entry. Each attachment has a `type` field (e.g. `"file"`, `"task"`, `"artifact"`) and type-specific additional fields. Empty array when there are no attachments.

automation_run.description: ID of the automation run that produced this entry (`atr_...`). `null` if not produced by an automation run.

content.description: A longer explanation of the event rendered as Markdown. `null` if no additional content is available.
content.example: The agent completed the task successfully.

correlation_id.description: An opaque string used to group related entries together. Entries sharing the same `correlation_id` belong to a single logical operation. `null` if not correlated.

created_at.description: When this activity feed entry was created (ISO 8601).

kind.description: The type of event this entry represents, e.g. `"agent_step"` or `"tool_call"`. Determines how `title`, `content`, and `attachments` should be interpreted.

level.description: Severity level of the event. One of `"info"`, `"warning"`, or `"error"`. `null` if no severity is set.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata stored on this entry. Returns an empty object when no metadata is set.

org.description: ID of the organization this entry belongs to (`org_...`). `null` if not org-scoped.

routine_run.description: ID of the agent routine run that produced this entry (`arr_...`). `null` if not produced by a routine run.

sandbox.description: Identifier of the sandbox environment this entry was generated in. `null` in production contexts.

session_record.description: ID of the agent session record this entry belongs to (`ase_...`). `null` if not part of an agent session.

team.description: ID of the team this entry is associated with (`tem_...`). `null` if not team-scoped.

thread.description: ID of the thread this entry is associated with (`thr_...`). `null` if not linked to a thread.

title.description: A one-line human-readable summary of the event. `null` if the entry has no title.

updated_at.description: When this activity feed entry was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: The user who triggered this event. Returns a user ID (`usr_...`) by default, or an expanded user object when the association is loaded. `null` if no user is associated.

oneOf.description: A platform user account. Represents a human or system actor that can own threads, belong to an organization, and interact with the API.

alias.description: Short handle or alias for the user. `null` if not set.

app.description: ID of the app this user (and their access token) is scoped to (`dap_...`). `null` if the user is not scoped to an app.

app_name.description: Display name of the user's app. `null` when the app association was not preloaded by the caller.

email.description: Email address of the user.

is_system_user.description: `true` if this account is an internal system user rather than a human. System users are created automatically by the platform.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the user. Defaults to an empty object.

name.description: Full display name of the user. `null` if the user has not set a name.

org.description: ID of the organization this user belongs to (`org_...`). `null` if the user is not a member of any organization.

org_name.description: Display name of the user's organization. `null` when the user is not in an org, or when the org association was not preloaded by the caller.

org_role.description: Role of the user within their organization. One of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"`. `null` when the user is not a member of any organization.

sandbox.description: ID of the sandbox environment this user is scoped to (`sbx_...`). `null` for production users.

sandbox_name.description: Display name of the user's sandbox environment. `null` for production users, or when the sandbox association was not preloaded by the caller.

ActivityFeedEntryListResponse.description: A paginated list of activity feed entries returned by a feed query, with cursors for navigating backward and forward through results.

entries.content: The agent completed the task successfully.
entries.title: Example Title

after_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as `after` to retrieve the next page of entries. `null` when this is the last page.

before_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as `before` to retrieve the previous page of entries. `null` when this is the first page.

entries.description: Array of activity feed entry objects for the current page, ordered by time descending.

has_more.description: Whether additional entries exist beyond the current page. When `true`, use `after_cursor` to fetch the next page.

Actor.description: The entity that authored a message, either a human user or an agent.

alias.description: Short handle or alias for the actor, used as an alternate display identifier. `null` if not configured.

id.description: Composite actor identifier. Format is `"user-<usr_...>"` for human users or `"agent-<agi_...>"` for agents.

name.description: Display name of the actor shown in the UI. `null` if no name is set.

profile_picture.description: Profile picture for the actor. `null` if the actor has no profile picture.

Agent.description: An AI agent that can be configured with tools, routines, and skills, and invoked to handle conversations or tasks.

example.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
example.name: Example Name

acl.description: Access control list for the agent. Contains a `grants` array where each entry specifies `principal_type`, `principal`, and `actions`. `null` when no ACL restrictions are applied and the agent is accessible to all members of its scope.

app.description: ID of the application that owns this agent (`dap_...`).

default_model.description: Default LLM model identifier used by this agent when no model is specified at runtime (e.g. `"claude-3-7-sonnet-latest"`).

email.description: Email address provisioned for this agent. `null` if email delivery is not configured.

identity.description: System-level identity prompt that shapes the agent's persona and behavior.
identity.example: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.

last_applied_template_config.description: ID of the AgentTemplate config (`cfg_...`) this agent was last provisioned or updated from. `null` for manually created agents.

lookup_key.description: Stable, user-defined identifier for this agent within the application. Unique per app.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the agent. Not interpreted by the platform.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the agent. `null` if not set.

org.description: ID of the organization this agent belongs to (`org_...`). `null` if the agent is not org-scoped.

org_name.description: Display name of the organization this agent belongs to. `null` when the agent is not org-scoped or when the org association was not preloaded.

originator.description: Free-form label identifying the source or author that created this agent (e.g. a username or pipeline name).

phone_number.description: Phone number provisioned for this agent. `null` if SMS is not configured.

sandbox.description: ID of the sandbox environment this agent is scoped to (`dsb_...`). `null` in production deployments.

source_solution.description: Source Solution and AgentTemplate summary for agents provisioned from a Solution. Includes `upgrade_available`, `latest_version`, and `latest_solution` so you can render an upgrade badge without a separate dry-run call. `null` for hand-built agents and agents whose tracked template or parent Solution has been deleted. Populated only on single-agent GET responses, never on list endpoints.

team.description: ID of the team that owns this agent (`tem_...`). `null` if the agent is not team-scoped.

updated_at.description: When the agent was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user that owns this agent (`usr_...`). `null` if the agent is not user-scoped.

AgentComputer.description: A cloud computer resource provisioned for an agent to use for browser and desktop automation tasks.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this computer (`agi_...`). `null` if the computer is not yet assigned to an agent.

app.description: ID of the app this computer belongs to (`dap_...`).

config.description: Provider-specific configuration key-value pairs for the computer. Structure depends on the underlying compute provider.

error_message.description: Human-readable error description when `status` is `"error"`. `null` otherwise.

last_active_at.description: When the computer last reported activity or received a command. `null` if the computer has never been active.

lookup_key.description: Unique, stable identifier you assign to this computer within its app. `null` if not set.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata you attached to the computer. `null` if none was provided.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the computer. `null` if not set.

region.description: Cloud region where the computer is hosted, e.g. `"us-east-1"`. `null` if not yet assigned.

sprite_url.description: URL of the live screenshot sprite used to render a real-time preview of the computer's screen. `null` when no sprite is available.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the computer. Common values include `"provisioning"`, `"ready"`, `"error"`, and `"terminated"`.

updated_at.description: When the computer record was last modified (ISO 8601).

AgentComputerListResponse.description: A list of agent computers returned by a list query.

data.description: Array of agent computer objects matching the query.

AgentCreateResponse.description: The response returned by `POST /api/v1/agents`. Contains all agent fields plus an optional `installed_configs` array when a `template_bundle` was supplied in the request.

example.identity: You are a helpful assistant.
example.name: Example Name

acl.description: Access control list governing who can interact with this agent. Contains a `grants` array where each entry specifies `principal_type`, `principal`, and `actions`. `null` when no ACL restrictions are applied.

app.description: ID of the app this agent belongs to (`dap_...`).

default_model.description: Default AI model the agent uses when no model is specified at runtime, e.g. `"claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022"`. `null` if not configured.

email.description: Email address assigned to this agent for inbound email handling. `null` if not configured.

identity.description: System prompt or persona description that shapes the agent's behavior. `null` if not set.

installed_configs.description: List of config records created as part of this request's `template_bundle` install. One entry per persisted config, sorted by `key`. Omitted entirely when the request did not include a `template_bundle`.

lookup_key.description: Unique, stable identifier for the agent within its app. `null` if not set.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the agent. `null` if none was provided.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the agent. `null` if not set.

org.description: ID of the organization this agent belongs to (`org_...`). `null` for agents outside an org.

originator.description: Free-form label identifying the source or author of the agent, e.g. a username or service name. `null` if not set.

phone_number.description: Phone number assigned to this agent for inbound SMS or voice handling. `null` if not configured.

sandbox.description: ID of the sandbox environment this agent is scoped to (`sbx_...`). `null` for agents not scoped to a sandbox.

team.description: ID of the team that owns this agent (`tea_...`). `null` if owned by a user rather than a team.

updated_at.description: When the agent record was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user that owns this agent (`usr_...`). `null` if owned by a team.

AgentEnvVarMasked.description: An agent environment variable with its secret value masked for safe display in list and show responses.

agent.description: ID of the agent this environment variable belongs to (`agt_...`).

created_at.description: When the environment variable was created (ISO 8601).

description.description: Optional human-readable note describing the purpose of this variable. `null` if not set.

key.description: Name of the environment variable as it appears in the agent's runtime.

masked_value.description: Redacted representation of the secret value. The last four characters are preserved; all preceding characters are replaced with `****`. Returns `****` when the value is absent or four characters or fewer.

updated_at.description: When the environment variable was last updated (ISO 8601).

AgentEnvVarMaskedList.description: Flat list of masked environment variables belonging to an agent.

data.description: An example description.
Array of masked environment variable objects for the agent.

AgentExport.description: A portable export bundle for an agent, containing everything needed to re-deploy it in another workspace or environment.

configs.description: Ordered list of config file objects that the agent depends on. Included in full so the import can recreate all dependencies without additional requests.

template.description: The agent template definition as a structured map. Pass this directly to the import endpoint to recreate the agent.

AgentHealth.description: Aggregate health profile for an agent, summarizing its current operational status, score, and the full list of setup and health actions.

agent.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
agent.name: Example Name

activity.description: Timestamps for the agent's most recent and next scheduled activity, used to surface last-run and upcoming-run information.

agent.description: The agent this health profile describes.

checked_at.description: When the health profile was last computed (ISO 8601).

checks.description: Renderable health check results. Each object includes at minimum `key`, `label`, `status`, and `summary` fields.

counts.description: Action counts broken down by dependency area and resolution status, used to render progress indicators per category.

health_actions.description: All actionable items tracked for this agent, including both `"setup"` items (post-install checklist) and `"health"` items (probe-detected issues). Sorted by `(source, sort_order, id)`. Use each item's `params` field to construct deep-links that route the user to the correct resolution flow.

recent.description: Recent execution metrics for the agent, including run counts and failure counts over a recent time window.

score.description: Normalized health score from `0` (fully degraded) to `100` (fully healthy), derived from the weight and status of all health actions.

status.description: Overall health status of the agent. One of `"ok"`, `"warning"`, or `"critical"`.

AgentHealthAction.description: A single actionable item in an agent's health or setup checklist, carrying the structured data needed to render the item and deep-link to the resolution flow.

agent.description: ID of the agent this action is scoped to (`agt_...`). `null` for org-level actions.

app.description: ID of the application this action is associated with (`app_...`). `null` when not app-scoped.

created_at.description: When this health action was first created (ISO 8601).

depends_on.description: IDs of other health actions that must reach `"completed"` status before this action can be started. Empty array when there are no dependencies.

description.description: Longer Markdown-formatted explanation of what the action requires and why. `null` if not provided.

kind.description: Category of action to take. One of `"env_var"` (set a secret), `"install"` (complete an agent installation, e.g. a GitHub App), `"custom"` (agent-defined step), or `"integration"` (authorize an OAuth-backed MCP server integration).

last_verified_at.description: When the verifier last ran for this action (ISO 8601). `null` until the verifier has been invoked at least once.

last_verifier_message.description: Human-readable output from the most recent verifier run. `null` if the verifier has not run yet.

org.description: ID of the organization this action is associated with (`org_...`). `null` when not org-scoped.

params.description: Kind-specific structured data used to construct the deep-link for this action. For `"env_var"` actions includes `key` and `scope`; for `"install"` actions includes `installation_kind`; for `"integration"` actions includes `mcp_server_ref`, and when resolvable also includes `provider` and `integration_id` for OAuth handoff. Empty object `{}` when no additional parameters are needed.

required.description: `true` if this action must be completed before the agent is considered fully operational and counts toward the blocking checklist progress bar.

sort_order.description: Display order within the same `source` group. Lower values appear first.

source.description: Lifecycle stage that produced this action. One of `"setup"` (post-install checklist item) or `"health"` (probe-detected issue).

status.description: Current resolution state. One of `"pending"` (not yet completed), `"completed"` (resolved), `"skipped"` (dismissed by the user), or `"degraded"` (completed but the verifier is reporting a warning).

title.description: Short display label for this action, intended for use as a checklist item heading.

updated_at.description: When this health action was last modified (ISO 8601).

verify_config.description: Configuration for the action's verifier step. Contains at minimum a `type` field that indicates which verification affordance to render. Server-internal fields are stripped before this is returned.

AgentListResponse.description: Paginated list of agent objects. Use the pagination fields to traverse multiple pages of results.

data.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
data.name: Example Name

data.description: Array of agent objects for the current page.

has_next.description: `true` when a subsequent page of results exists.

has_prev.description: `true` when a previous page of results exists.

page_size.description: Maximum number of agents returned per page.

total_entries.description: Total number of agents matching the query across all pages.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages available given the current `page_size`.

AgentRoutine.description: An agent routine defines a reusable handler — script, preset, or chain — that runs in response to events or on a schedule.

preset_config.instructions: You are a helpful assistant. Answer questions concisely and cite sources when possible.

acl.description: Access control list for the routine. Contains a `grants` array where each entry specifies `principal_type`, `principal`, and `actions`. `null` when no ACL restrictions are applied and the routine is accessible to all members of its scope.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this routine (`agi_...`).

app.description: Application that scopes this routine (`dap_...`).

config.description: ID of the Config record that backs this routine's configuration (`cfg_...`). `null` when the routine is not config-backed.

description.description: Optional description of what this routine does. `null` when not set.

event_config.description: Additional configuration controlling how the event trigger is matched or filtered. Shape depends on `event_type`. `null` when not configured.

event_type.description: Platform event type that triggers this routine, e.g. `"agentroutine.invoked"`. `null` for schedule-only routines.

handler_type.description: Execution strategy for this routine. One of `"workflow_graph"`, `"script"`, `"preset"`, or `"chain"`.

last_applied_template_config.description: ID of the AgentRoutineTemplate Config this routine was last provisioned or updated from (`cfg_...`). `null` for hand-built routines.

lookup_key.description: Unique human-readable key used to look up this routine without knowing its ID. `null` when not set.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this routine. `null` when not set.

name.description: Human-readable name for the routine.

preset_config.description: Resolved preset configuration when `handler_type` is `"preset"`. `null` for other handler types.

preset_name.description: Name of the preset invoked when `handler_type` is `"preset"`. `null` for other handler types.

schedule.description: Cron expression controlling when the routine fires on a schedule. `null` for event-only routines.

script.description: Inline script body executed when `handler_type` is `"script"`. `null` for other handler types.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the routine. One of `"draft"`, `"active"`, or `"paused"`. Only `"active"` routines respond to triggers.

steps.description: Ordered list of chain steps (present when handler_type is "chain"). Each step is a plain map with handler_type, optional body fields (preset_name / preset_config / script / config), and step-local plumbing (name, inputs, output_key, on_error).

trigger_context.description: Execution context in which runs are created. One of `"event"` (background job) or `"chat_session"` (interactive session). Defaults to `"event"`.

updated_at.description: When this routine was last updated (ISO 8601).

AgentRoutineListResponse.description: List of agent routine objects belonging to a given agent.

data.description: An example description.
data.name: Example Name

preset_config.instructions: You are a helpful assistant. Answer questions concisely and cite sources when possible.

data.description: Array of agent routine objects.

AgentRoutineRun.description: A single execution of an agent routine, capturing its status, inputs, outputs, and timing.

acl.description: Access control list for the run. Contains a `grants` array where each entry specifies `principal_type`, `principal`, and `actions`. `null` when no ACL restrictions are applied and the run is accessible to all members of its scope.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns the parent routine (`agi_...`).

app.description: Application that scopes this run (`dap_...`).

duration_ms.description: Total wall-clock time the run took to execute, in milliseconds. `null` while the run is still in progress.

event_id.description: Identifier of the platform event that triggered this run. `null` for manually invoked runs.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this run. Empty object when no metadata was set.

payload.description: Input payload delivered to the routine when this run was triggered. Empty object when no payload was provided.

result.description: Output produced by the routine after execution. `null` while the run has not yet completed.

routine.description: ID of the parent routine that produced this run (`arn_...`).

status.description: Current execution status. One of `"pending"`, `"running"`, `"completed"`, `"failed"`, `"skipped"`, or `"cancelled"`.

structured_response.description: Validated structured output extracted from `result` when the routine uses an AgentMessageSchema. `null` if the routine does not use a schema or the run has not completed.

updated_at.description: When this run was last updated (ISO 8601).

worker.description: Background worker status. `null` when no worker job is associated with this run.

AgentRoutineRunListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list of agent routine run objects, ordered by creation time descending.

after_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as the after-cursor parameter to fetch the next page of runs. `null` when no later results exist.

before_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as the before-cursor parameter to fetch the page of runs that precede this one. `null` when no earlier results exist.

data.description: Array of agent routine run objects for the current page.

AgentSchedule.description: A scheduled task created by an agent. Supports one-time and recurring (cron-based) execution patterns.

example.instructions: Send a daily summary of open support tickets to the team Slack channel.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this schedule (`agi_...`).

app.description: ID of the application the schedule belongs to (`dap_...`).

cron_expression.description: Standard cron expression defining the recurrence pattern (e.g. `"0 9 * * 1"`). Present only when `schedule_type` is `"recurring"`. `null` for one-time schedules.

instructions.description: The task description the agent will execute when this schedule fires.
instructions.example: Send a daily summary of open support tickets to the team Slack channel.

last_run_at.description: UTC datetime of the most recent successful execution. `null` if the schedule has never run.

max_runs.description: Maximum number of times a recurring schedule may fire before automatically transitioning to `"completed"`. `null` means no limit.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs attached to the schedule by the agent. Not interpreted by the platform.

next_run_at.description: UTC datetime of the next planned execution. `null` if the schedule has completed, been cancelled, or has not yet been computed.

run_count.description: Total number of times this schedule has fired.

schedule_type.description: Determines how the schedule repeats. `"once"` fires a single time at `scheduled_at` then transitions to `"completed"`. `"recurring"` fires on the `cron_expression` and reschedules automatically.

scheduled_at.description: The exact UTC datetime at which a one-time schedule fires. Present only when `schedule_type` is `"once"`. `null` for recurring schedules.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the schedule. One of `"active"` (will fire as planned), `"paused"` (temporarily suspended), `"completed"` (has run its last execution), `"cancelled"` (manually stopped), or `"expired"` (past its valid window).

thread.description: Thread ID (`thr_...`) this schedule is bound to. When set, the scheduled task is delivered into the thread rather than creating a new session. `null` for session-based schedules.

timezone.description: IANA timezone name used to interpret the cron expression or `scheduled_at` (e.g. `"America/New_York"`). Defaults to `"Etc/UTC"`.

updated_at.description: When the schedule was last modified (ISO 8601).

AgentSession.description: A durable agent session record representing a single AI task execution. Tracks status, trajectory, result, and any inbox messages delivered to the session.

example.instructions: Summarize the latest activity report and post the results to Slack.
example.name: Example Name

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this session (`agi_...`).

completed_at.description: When the session reached a terminal state (`"completed"`, `"failed"`, or `"cancelled"`). `null` if still in progress.

error.description: Human-readable error message describing why the session failed. `null` unless `status` is `"failed"`.

inbox.description: Ordered list of messages delivered to the session's inbox while it was in the `"waiting"` state. Each entry includes `id`, `role`, `content`, `sender_id`, `sender_type`, `sent_at`, and `metadata`.

instructions.description: The task the agent is instructed to perform in this session.
instructions.example: Summarize the latest activity report and post the results to Slack.

is_system_session.description: `true` if this session was created by the platform internally (e.g. by a schedule or health action) rather than by a user or API caller.

max_runs_per_turn.description: Maximum number of tool calls the agent may make within a single turn. Defaults to `25`.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens the session may consume across all turns before being terminated. Defaults to `20000`.

max_turns.description: Maximum number of agent turns (LLM calls) allowed before the session is forcibly terminated. Defaults to `100`.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs attached to the session. Not interpreted by the platform.

name.description: Optional human-readable label for the session. `null` when not set.

result.description: Structured output produced by the session on successful completion. Shape is agent-defined. `null` while the session is still running or if it failed.

started_at.description: When the session began executing. `null` if still `"pending"`.

status.description: Current execution status. One of `"pending"` (queued, not yet started), `"running"` (actively executing), `"waiting"` (paused for an inbox message or external event), `"completed"` (finished successfully), `"failed"` (terminated with an error), or `"cancelled"` (manually stopped).

trajectory.description: ID of the trajectory that records the full message history for this session (`trj_...`). `null` until the session has started.

AgentSessionListResponse.description: Paginated list response containing an array of agent session objects.

data.instructions: Summarize the latest activity report and post the results to Slack.
data.name: Example Name

data.description: Array of agent session objects for the current page.

AgentSkill.description: A skill enabled on an agent, linking the agent to a skill configuration. Controls which capabilities the agent has access to.

agent.description: ID of the agent this skill is attached to (`agi_...`).

app.description: ID of the application this skill belongs to (`dap_...`).

config.description: ID of the root skill config record that defines this skill's behavior (`cfg_...`).

created_at.description: When the skill was added to the agent (ISO 8601).

instruction.description: Optional instruction text that overrides the default skill instructions for this specific agent. `null` when no override is set.

last_applied_template_config.description: ID of the agent template config from which this skill was last provisioned or updated (`cfg_...`). `null` if the skill was not provisioned from a template.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs attached to the skill. Not interpreted by the platform.

status.description: Whether the skill is currently in use. `"active"` means the agent will use this skill during sessions. `"inactive"` means it is disabled but not deleted.

updated_at.description: When the skill was last modified (ISO 8601).

AgentSkillList.description: Paginated list response containing an array of agent skill objects.

data.description: Array of agent skill objects for the current page.

AgentSourceSolution.description:
Summary of the Solution and AgentTemplate that an agent was last provisioned from.
Returned on single-agent responses; `null` for hand-built agents and agents whose tracked template or parent Solution has been deleted.

solution.description: Summary of the parent Solution, including `upgrade_available`, `latest_version`, and `latest_solution` when a newer system-scoped version is available for the agent's org-scoped Solution.

template.description: Summary of the AgentTemplate config (`cfg_...`) the agent was last provisioned or updated from.

AgentTool.description: A tool attached to an agent, defining a capability the agent can invoke during a conversation or task run.

agent.description: ID of the agent this tool belongs to (`agi_...`).

app.description: ID of the application that owns this tool (`dap_...`).

async.description: `true` when the tool executes asynchronously and returns a task handle rather than an immediate result.

builtin_tool_config.description: Provider-specific configuration for the built-in tool. Present only when `kind` is `"builtin"`. Shape varies by `builtin_tool_key`.

builtin_tool_key.description: Registry key identifying the built-in tool implementation. Present only when `kind` is `"builtin"`.

config.description: ID of the config record (`cfg_...`) containing this tool's full configuration. `null` for inline-only tools.

description.description: Description of what the tool does, passed to the LLM as part of the tool definition. Resolved from the built-in registry for `kind: "builtin"` tools.

handler_type.description: Execution handler type. One of `"http"`, `"script"`, or `"builtin"`.

instruction.description: Optional system-level instruction appended to the agent prompt when this tool is active.

kind.description: Tool kind. One of `"builtin"`, `"custom"`, or `"mcp"`.

last_applied_template_config.description: ID of the AgentToolTemplate config (`cfg_...`) this tool was last provisioned or updated from. `null` for manually created tools.

lookup_key.description: Stable, user-defined identifier for this tool within the agent. Unique per agent.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the tool. Not interpreted by the platform.

name.description: Human-readable name of the tool as exposed to the LLM. Resolved from the built-in registry for `kind: "builtin"` tools.

name_prefix.description: Per-instance namespace prepended to LLM-facing tool names for built-in tools that support multiple instances per agent. `null` when not applicable.

parameters.description: JSON Schema object describing the tool's input parameters as presented to the LLM.

parameters_config.description: ID of the config record (`cfg_...`) storing the tool's parameter schema. `null` when parameters are defined inline.

status.description: Current status of the tool. One of `"active"` or `"disabled"`.

updated_at.description: When the tool was last modified (ISO 8601).

AgentToolListResponse.description: Paginated list response containing the tools attached to an agent.

data.description: An example description.
data.name: Example Name

data.description: Array of agent tool objects returned for the current request.

AgentUpgradeChange.description: One child-resource change produced by an agent upgrade, describing the action to be taken on a single resource.

action.description: The operation that will be performed. One of `"add"`, `"update"`, `"remove"`, or `"noop"`.

description.description: Description of the child resource this change touches, when one is set. `null` when no description is available.

field_changes.description: Field-level diff entries for this change. Populated only when `action` is `"update"`; empty or absent for `add`, `remove`, and `noop` entries.

id.description: Public ID of the existing resource being updated or removed (e.g. `atl_...`, `arn_...`). `null` for `add` entries.

key.description: Lookup key of the resource derived from its source template. `null` when the template has no lookup key.

name.description: Human-facing name of the child resource this change touches (tool/routine/skill/computer name, or builtin tool key for unnamed builtin tools). Falls back to the source template's name. `null` for the synthetic `agent_base` entry.

parent_template_config.description: Summary of the parent AgentTemplate config (`cfg_...`) being applied in this upgrade.

resource.description: Resource-type-specific identity details. Tools: `tool_type`, `builtin_tool_key`, `name_prefix`, `handler_type`, `instruction`. Routines: `handler_type`, `preset_name`, `event_type`, `schedule`, `trigger_context`. Skills: `instruction`. Computers: `region`. Only populated keys are present; `null` when nothing is known.

resource_type.description: Type of the child resource being changed. One of `"agent"`, `"tool"`, `"routine"`, `"skill"`, or `"computer"`.

source_template_config.description: Summary of the specific child template config (`cfg_...`) that defines this resource. `null` when no source template is resolvable.

AgentUpgradeFieldChange.description:
One field-level diff entry within an agent upgrade change, describing how a single field will change.
`baseline` and `locally_edited` are populated only for `agent_base` entries; child resource entries (tools, routines, skills, computers) carry only `field`, `old`, and `new`.

baseline.description: Value that was set by the last-applied template version (pinned baseline). Populated only on `agent_base` field changes. `null` when no baseline is available (legacy agent or deleted version).

field.description: Name of the field that will change, e.g. `"name"` or `"identity"`.

locally_edited.description: `true` when the agent's current value differs from `baseline`, indicating a local edit that this upgrade will overwrite. `false` when the current value matches the baseline. `null` when `baseline` is unavailable. Populated only on `agent_base` field changes.

new.description: Incoming value the field will be set to after the upgrade (string, number, boolean, or `null`).

old.description: Current value of the field before the upgrade (string, number, boolean, or `null`).

AgentUpgradeResponse.description: Response returned by the agent upgrade endpoint, combining the updated agent, its source Solution and template, and the full upgrade diff.

agent.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
agent.name: Example Name

agent.description: The agent after the upgrade has been applied. `null` for dry-run requests where no changes were persisted.

solution.description: Summary of the parent Solution the agent was upgraded from.

template.description: Summary of the AgentTemplate config (`cfg_...`) that was selected for this upgrade.

upgrade_result.description: Full upgrade diff including status, mode, dry-run flag, summary counts, and per-resource change list.

AgentUpgradeResult.description: The computed diff and outcome of an agent upgrade operation, including the full list of per-resource changes.

changes.description: Ordered list of per-resource changes that will be (or were) applied by this upgrade.

dry_run.description: `true` when the request was a dry run and no changes were persisted to the agent.

mode.description: Upgrade mode that was used. One of `"full"` (apply all changes) or `"review"` (require fingerprint confirmation).

review_fingerprint.description: Opaque fingerprint of the computed diff. Pass this value back as `review_fingerprint` to confirm and apply a `"review"` mode upgrade.

status.description: Outcome of the upgrade. `"ready"` for a dry-run (no changes applied); `"upgraded"` when the upgrade was committed.

summary.description: Aggregate counts of adds, updates, removes, and noops across all child resources.

AgentUpgradeSummary.description: Aggregate counts of each change type produced by an agent upgrade diff.

adds.description: Number of child resources that will be created by this upgrade.

noops.description: Number of child resources with no changes in this upgrade.

removes.description: Number of child resources that will be removed by this upgrade.

updates.description: Number of child resources that will be updated by this upgrade.

Artifact.description: A versioned artifact produced or managed by an agent, such as a generated file, report, or code output.

agent.description: ID of the agent that produced this artifact (`agt_...`). `null` if not agent-produced.

content_type.description: MIME type of the current version's file, e.g. `"text/csv"` or `"image/png"`. `null` if no file is attached.

created_at.description: When the artifact was first created (ISO 8601).

current_version.description: ID of the current (latest published) artifact version (`artv_...`). `null` if no version has been published.

description.description: Optional longer description of the artifact's contents or purpose. `null` if not set.

file.description: Storage file ID for the current version (`fil_...`). `null` if no file is attached.

file_name.description: Original filename of the current version's file, e.g. `"output.csv"`. `null` if no file is attached.

file_url.description: Short-lived signed URL for downloading the current version's file. `null` if no file is attached.

image_source.description: Image source metadata for rendering the current version's file inline. Present only when `content_type` starts with `"image/"`. `null` otherwise.

name.description: Human-readable name for the artifact, e.g. `"Q2 Report"`. `null` if not set.

org.description: ID of the organization this artifact belongs to (`org_...`).

sandbox.description: Identifier of the sandbox environment associated with this artifact. `null` if not sandbox-scoped.

team.description: ID of the team that owns this artifact (`tea_...`). `null` if not team-scoped.

thread.description: ID of the thread in which this artifact was created (`thr_...`). `null` if not thread-scoped.

updated_at.description: When the artifact record was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user who created this artifact (`usr_...`). `null` if not user-scoped.

version.description: Current version number of the artifact. Increments each time a new version is published.

Attachment.description: A rich attachment associated with a message, such as a file, scraped link, artifact, task, media item, or inline action.

content_type.description: MIME type of the attached file, e.g. `"image/png"` or `"application/pdf"`. Present on `file`, `artifact`, and `media` types. `null` otherwise.

description.description: Short description. The page meta-description for `scraped_link`, the artifact description for `artifact`, and the task description for `task` types. `null` on other types.

filename.description: Original filename of the attached file, e.g. `"report.pdf"`. Present on `file`, `artifact`, and `media` types. `null` otherwise.

height.description: Height in pixels of the media item. Present on `media` type only. `null` otherwise.

id.description: Unique identifier for this attachment within the message.

image_height.description: Height in pixels of the scraped preview image. Present on `scraped_link` type only. `null` otherwise.

image_source.description: Image source metadata for inline rendering. Present on `file`, `scraped_link`, `artifact`, and `media` types when the content is an image. `null` otherwise.

image_url.description: URL of the preview image extracted from the scraped page. Present on `scraped_link` type only. `null` otherwise.

image_width.description: Width in pixels of the scraped preview image. Present on `scraped_link` type only. `null` otherwise.

media_type.description: The media category, e.g. `"video"` or `"audio"`. Present on `media` type only. `null` otherwise.

name.description: Display name of the media item. Present on `media` type only. `null` otherwise.

object.description: The full embedded object payload. For `task` type, contains the task record. For `action` type, contains the action definition. `null` on other types.

title.description: Display title. The page title for `scraped_link`, the artifact name for `artifact`, and the task title for `task` types. `null` on other types.

type.description: The attachment type. One of `"file"`, `"scraped_link"`, `"artifact"`, `"task"`, `"media"`, or `"action"`. Determines which additional fields are present.

url.description: URL to access the resource. A signed download URL for `file` and `artifact` types; the original URL for `scraped_link`; a media playback URL for `media`. `null` on `task` and `action` types.

variants.description: Array of available encoding variants for the media item (e.g. different resolutions). Present on `media` type only. `null` otherwise.

version.description: Version number of the attached artifact at the time of attachment. Present on `artifact` type only. `null` otherwise.

width.description: Width in pixels of the media item. Present on `media` type only. `null` otherwise.

AuthTokens.description: Credential bundle returned after a successful authentication exchange. Contains the access token, refresh token, and the authenticated user.

expires_in.description: Number of seconds until `token` expires. After this period, use `refresh_token` to obtain a new access token.

metadata.description: Optional auxiliary data associated with this authentication event, such as `onboarding_job_id` when the user is completing onboarding. `null` when no extra context is present.

refresh_token.description: Long-lived opaque refresh token. Use this to obtain a new access token when `token` expires.

token.description: Short-lived JWT access token. Include this value in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for all authenticated API requests.

user.description: The user who authenticated. Contains the user's profile and account details.

AutomationRun.description: A single execution of an automation triggered by a platform event or direct invocation. Captures the run's status, input payload, and final result.

app.description: ID of the app that owns this automation run (`dap_...`).

automation.description: ID of the automation that was executed (`aut_...`).

created_at.description: When the automation run was created (ISO 8601).

event_id.description: ID of the platform event that triggered this run. `null` for directly invoked automations.

payload.description: The input event payload that triggered this run. Structure varies by automation type. Defaults to an empty object if no payload was provided.

result.description: The output produced after the automation finished executing. Contains workflow-defined keys alongside any returned output. `null` if the run has not yet completed.

status.description: Current execution status of the run. One of `"pending"` (queued, not yet started), `"running"` (actively executing), `"completed"` (finished successfully), `"failed"` (finished with an error), or `"cancelled"` (stopped before completion).

team.description: ID of the team that owns this run (`tea_...`). `null` if the run is owned by a user rather than a team.

updated_at.description: When the automation run record was last updated (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user that owns this run (`usr_...`). `null` if the run is owned by a team rather than a user.

BugReport.description: A bug report or freeform feedback submission from any ArchAstro client. Bug reports are write-only for the submitting user and are not returned by any public list or show endpoint.

app.description: App ID (`dap_...`) of the developer app through which the report was submitted.

client.description: The client application that submitted this report. One of `"agent_network_web"`, `"cli"`, or `"developer_portal"`.

client_version.description: Version string of the submitting client at the time of submission, e.g. `"1.4.2"`.

context.description: Optional free-form JSON object providing additional context captured by the client (e.g. viewport size, active route). `null` when no context was provided. Maximum 5 KB when serialized.

created_at.description: When the bug report was submitted (ISO 8601).

description.description: Freeform text describing the issue or feedback, as entered by the user. Up to 10,000 characters.

org.description: Organization ID (`org_...`) scoping this report. `null` when the user's account is not part of an organization.

sandbox.description: Sandbox ID (`dsb_...`) active at submission time. `null` when the report was not submitted from a sandbox context.

team.description: Team ID (`tem_...`) of the team the submitting user belonged to at submission time. `null` when the user had no active team.

updated_at.description: When the bug report record was last modified (ISO 8601).

BuiltinTool.description: A single callable tool within a builtin tool catalog entry. Represents one discrete function an agent can invoke.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what the tool does. Surfaced to the agent as part of tool selection context. `null` when no description has been defined.

name.description: Machine-readable name of the tool as it is registered with the agent runtime, e.g. `"web_search"` or `"github_create_issue"`.

BuiltinToolCatalogEntry.description: A catalog entry describing a category of platform-provided (builtin) tools that can be enabled for an agent. Each entry groups one or more individual tools under a shared key, label, and configuration schema.

config_schema.description: JSON Schema object describing the configuration options for this tool category. Clients should use this schema to render and validate configuration forms before submitting. `null` when no configuration is needed.

description.description: Short prose description of what this tool category does. Suitable for display in setup UIs. `null` when no description has been defined.

instruction.description: Additional guidance surfaced to the agent at runtime when this tool category is enabled. `null` when no custom instruction is set.

key.description: Unique slug identifying this tool category, e.g. `"web_search"` or `"github"`.

label.description: Human-readable display name for the tool category, e.g. `"Web Search"`. `null` when no label has been assigned.

multi_instance_mode.description: Controls whether multiple instances of this tool category may be enabled simultaneously. `"namespaced"` — multiple instances allowed; each must carry a `name_prefix` to distinguish them. `"passthrough"` — multiple instances allowed without a `name_prefix`; names are derived from the underlying source. `null` — single-instance only.

providers.description: List of integration provider slugs that can back this tool category, e.g. `["github", "gitlab"]`. Empty when the tool is provider-agnostic.

requires_integration.description: Whether enabling this tool category requires the user to connect a third-party integration. `true` means at least one active integration of the appropriate type must exist before the tool can be used.

server_tool_type.description: Internal type identifier used by the platform server when registering these tools. `null` for client-side-only tool categories.

tools.description: Array of individual tool definitions included in this category. Each entry describes a single callable tool with its own name and description.

ChannelAck.description: Empty acknowledgement payload returned by channel message handlers that produce no data. The wire envelope is `{"status": "ok", "response": {}}`.

ChatForkThreadResponse.description: Response returned after forking a chat thread. Contains the new thread, its initial chat-room snapshot, and the owning team when applicable.

chat_model.description: Initial chat-room render snapshot for the forked thread, including members and loaded messages. `null` for transient threads whose room model is suppressed.

team.description: Team that owns the forked thread. Present only when the original thread was team-scoped; `null` for personal threads.

thread.description: The newly-created thread produced by the fork operation.

ChatLoadMoreMessagesResponse.description: Response returned after loading an additional page of chat messages. Contains a refreshed chat-room snapshot with the newly-fetched messages merged in.

data.description: Updated chat-room snapshot for the thread, incorporating the newly-loaded page of messages alongside any previously loaded messages.

ChatMarkThreadReadResponse.description: Response returned after marking a chat thread as read. Confirms that the read marker was successfully recorded for the authenticated user.

success.description: Indicates whether the read marker was successfully applied. Always `true` on success; errors are returned as channel error replies rather than a `false` value here.

ChatMember.description: A participant in a chat thread, which may be either a human user or an AI agent. Exactly one of `user` or `agent` is populated depending on `type`.

agent.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
agent.name: Example Name

agent.description: Full agent object for this member. Populated when `type` is `"agent"`; `null` for user members.

membership_type.description: Role of this member within the thread. Common values are `"owner"` and `"member"`. `null` when the membership type is not applicable.

type.description: Kind of participant. One of `"user"` (a human user) or `"agent"` (an AI agent).

user.description: Full user object for this member. Populated when `type` is `"user"`; `null` for agent members.

ChatMessageListResponse.description: Response returned when listing the messages of a joined chat thread. Contains the set of messages currently loaded for the thread.

messages.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

messages.description: Ordered array of message objects currently loaded for the thread, from oldest to newest. Use the `load_more_messages` channel message to fetch earlier pages.

ChatPostMessageResponse.description: Response returned after successfully posting a message to a chat thread. Contains the persisted message object echoed back to the sender.

message.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

message.description: The message that was created and stored. Contains the full message object including its assigned ID, author, content, and timestamps.

ChatRoomModel.description: A point-in-time snapshot of a chat room's state, including its loaded messages, member roster, and pagination cursors. Returned when loading or refreshing a thread's message list.

agent.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
agent.name: Example Name

messages.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

team.description: An example description.
team.name: Example Name

thread.description: An example description.

parent_message.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

participating_agents.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
participating_agents.name: Example Name

thread.title: Example Title

after_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass when fetching messages newer than those in this snapshot. `null` when this snapshot already reflects the latest messages.

agent.description: The agent associated with this chat room. `null` when no agent is attached.

before_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass when fetching messages older than those in this snapshot. `null` when the beginning of the thread history has been reached.

is_transient.description: Whether this thread is ephemeral. Transient threads are not retained in long-term storage and may be deleted when the session ends.

members.description: All active members of the chat room, including both human users and agents.

messages.description: The page of messages currently loaded for the thread, ordered chronologically. Use `before_cursor` or `after_cursor` to page through additional history.

messages_loaded_on_last_update.description: Number of messages that were added to the snapshot in the most recent incremental update. `null` on the initial load.

team.description: The team that owns this thread. `null` for threads scoped to an individual user rather than a team.

thread.description: The parent thread whose message history and membership this snapshot represents.

ComputerExecResult.description: The result of executing a shell command on an agent's computer environment. Contains the captured output and the process exit code.

exit_code.description: The UNIX exit code returned by the process. `0` indicates success; any non-zero value indicates an error. `null` if the process did not terminate normally.

output.description: The combined stdout and stderr output produced by the command. `null` if the command produced no output.

Config.description: A versioned config file owned by a team or user, representing a typed artifact such as an agent definition or API tool specification.

agent.description: Agent ID (`agt_...`) associated with this config. `null` if not linked to an agent.

created_at.description: When this config was first created (ISO 8601).

current_version.description: The most recently saved version of this config. `null` if the config has never been saved with content.

is_archived.description: Whether this config has been archived. Archived configs are hidden from default listings but remain accessible by ID.

kind.description: Type of config, e.g. `"Agent"` or `"APITool"`. Determines which fields and validation rules apply.

lookup_key.description: Stable, user-defined key used to look up this config without knowing its ID. `null` if not set.

mime_type.description: MIME type of the config's content, e.g. `"text/yaml"`. `null` if not determined.

org.description: Organization ID (`org_...`) this config belongs to. `null` for configs not scoped to an org.

parent.description: Parent bundle config ID (`cfg_...`). Present only for configs that are children of a bundle; `null` otherwise.

parent_solution.description: ID (`cfg_...`) of the solution config this config was imported with. `null` if the config was not imported via a solution.

raw_content.description: Raw file content as a string. Populated only for system configs; `null` for user-owned configs.

relative_path.description: Path of this config relative to its parent bundle root. Present only for bundle children; `null` otherwise.

sandbox.description: Sandbox identifier this config belongs to. `null` for production configs.

team.description: Team ID (`tea_...`) that owns this config. `null` for personal (user-scoped) configs.

updated_at.description: When this config was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: User ID (`usr_...`) who owns this config. `null` for team-scoped configs.

virtual_path.description: Logical path uniquely identifying this config within its team, e.g. `"agents/my-agent.yaml"`. `null` for configs without an explicit path.

ConfigFacets.description: Aggregated facet counts for the authenticated viewer's configs. Reports all distinct kinds and path prefixes across the full dataset, regardless of any active list filters.

kinds.description: All distinct config kinds present in the viewer's dataset, each with its total count. Use these values to populate kind filter options.

path_prefixes.description: All distinct slash-terminated path prefixes present in the viewer's dataset, each with its total count. Use these values to populate path prefix filter options.

ConfigKindFacet.description: A facet entry grouping configs by kind, returning the kind name and the number of matching configs visible to the authenticated viewer.

count.description: Number of configs of this kind that are visible to the authenticated viewer. Always `0` or greater.

kind.description: The config kind identifier (e.g., `"Agent"`, `"WorkflowGraph"`). Matches the `kind` field on config objects.

ConfigKindSchema.description: The JSON Schema definition and sample YAML for a specific config kind, used to validate and scaffold new configs of that kind.

json_schema.description: JSON Schema object describing the valid structure of a config of this kind. `null` when no schema has been registered for this kind.
