kind.description: The config kind identifier (e.g., `"Agent"`, `"WorkflowGraph"`). Matches the `kind` field on config objects.

sample_yaml.description: A sample YAML document illustrating a minimal valid config of this kind. `null` when no sample has been registered for this kind.

ConfigPathPrefixFacet.description: A facet entry grouping configs by their leading path segment, returning the prefix and the number of matching configs visible to the authenticated viewer.

count.description: Number of configs whose `virtual_path` begins with this prefix that are visible to the authenticated viewer. Always `0` or greater.

prefix.description: The leading path segment of the config's `virtual_path`, always slash-terminated (e.g., `"agents/"`, `"__editor/"`). Pass this value as the `path_prefix` filter to narrow config listings.

ConfigVersion.description: A single immutable snapshot of a config's content, created each time the config is saved.

change_description.description: Human-readable summary of what changed in this version, as provided by the author. `null` if no description was supplied.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 digest of the raw config content encoded as `sha256:<hex>`. Uses the same algorithm as the CLI `computeContentHash` helper. `null` for versions created before this field was introduced.

created_at.description: When this config version was created (ISO 8601).

data.description: Arbitrary structured metadata stored alongside this version. `null` when no extra data was provided.

org.description: Organization ID (`org_...`) that owns this config version. `null` for personal configs.

sandbox.description: Sandbox ID (`sbx_...`) this version was saved under. `null` for production configs.

version_number.description: Monotonically increasing integer identifying this version within the config. Starts at 1.

ContextDocument.description: A context document stored within a context source. Carries metadata and size information only; retrieve the full text content via the `/content` endpoint.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this document (`agi_...`). `null` if owned by a user or team.

file.description: ID of the backing storage file (`fil_...`) when the document is file-backed. `null` for inline documents.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the document. Shape varies by source type.

source.description: ID of the context source this document belongs to (`cso_...`).

team.description: ID of the team that owns this document (`tem_...`). `null` if owned by a user or agent.

title.description: Human-readable display title of the document. `null` if no title has been set.

total_lines.description: Total number of lines in the document's text content. `0` if the document has no content.

total_size.description: Total byte size of the document's text content. `0` if the document has no content.

updated_at.description: When the document was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user that owns this document (`usr_...`). `null` if owned by a team or agent.

ContextDocumentContent.description: The text content of a context document, optionally sliced by line or byte range. Includes totals and slice boundary fields for the requested unit.

example.content: This is the document content.
example.title: Example Title

content.description: Text of the document. Contains the full content when no `offset`/`limit` was requested, or only the requested slice otherwise.

end_byte.description: Zero-based exclusive index of the last byte in `content` (i.e. the slice covers bytes `start_byte..end_byte-1`). Populated only when `unit` is `"bytes"`; `null` otherwise.

end_line.description: 1-indexed line number of the last line included in `content` (i.e. the slice covers lines `start_line` through `end_line` inclusive). Populated only when `unit` is `"lines"`; `null` otherwise.

limit.description: The `limit` value echoed from the request. `null` when no limit was requested.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the document, such as source URL or author. `null` if no metadata was recorded.

offset.description: The `offset` value echoed from the request. `null` when no offset was requested.

start_byte.description: Zero-based index of the first byte included in `content`. Populated only when `unit` is `"bytes"`; `null` otherwise.

start_line.description: 1-indexed line number of the first line included in `content`. Populated only when `unit` is `"lines"`; `null` otherwise.

title.description: Human-readable display title of the document. `null` if the document has no title set.

total_lines.description: Total number of lines in the document's full content, regardless of any slice.

total_size.description: Total byte size of the document's full content, regardless of any slice.

unit.description: Slice unit used when `offset` and `limit` were provided. One of `"lines"` (default) or `"bytes"`. `null` when no slice was requested.

ContextIngestion.description: A context ingestion job that processes a context source and populates its documents. Tracks status and timing from submission through completion or failure.

agent.description: ID of the agent that initiated this ingestion (`agi_...`). `null` if initiated by a user.

completed_at.description: When the ingestion job finished, either successfully or with a failure. `null` if still in progress.

error.description: Structured error details when the ingestion has `status: "failed"`. `null` for any other status.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata associated with this ingestion run. Shape is caller-defined.

source.description: ID of the context source being ingested (`cso_...`). `null` if the source has been deleted.

started_at.description: When the ingestion job began processing. `null` if the job is still pending.

status.description: Current processing status. One of `"pending"`, `"running"`, `"awaiting_callback"`, `"succeeded"`, or `"failed"`.

team.description: ID of the team that owns this ingestion (`tem_...`). `null` if owned by a user or agent.

updated_at.description: When the ingestion record was last updated (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user that initiated this ingestion (`usr_...`). `null` if initiated by an agent.

CustomObject.description: A custom object belonging to an organization. Custom objects store arbitrary structured data defined by a schema type and are scoped to an org, team, or user.

created_at.description: When the custom object was created (ISO 8601).

fields.description: Map of field names to their current values as defined by the object's schema type.

id.description: Unique identifier for the custom object (`cobj_...`).

org.description: ID of the organization this object belongs to (`org_...`).

row_key.description: An optional stable key used to identify this object by a caller-controlled string rather than its generated ID. `null` if not set.

sandbox.description: ID of the sandbox environment this object is scoped to (`dsb_...`). `null` for production objects.

schema_type.description: The lookup key of the schema type that defines this object's field structure. `null` if the schema type has not been set.

team.description: ID of the team that owns this object (`tem_...`). `null` if the object is not team-scoped.

updated_at.description: When the custom object was last modified (ISO 8601). `null` if the object has never been updated after creation.

user.description: ID of the user that owns this object (`usr_...`). `null` if the object is not user-scoped.

version.description: Optimistic concurrency version of the object. Increments with each successful update; pass this value in write operations to detect conflicting changes.

CustomObjectListResponse.description: A paginated page of custom objects returned by a list operation. Use the pagination fields to navigate through result sets.

data.description: Array of custom objects for the current page.

has_next.description: `true` if a subsequent page of results exists; `false` if this is the last page.

has_prev.description: `true` if a preceding page of results exists; `false` if this is the first page.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results returned per page.

total_entries.description: Total number of custom objects matching the query across all pages.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages available for the current query.

CustomObjectUpdateFieldsResponse.description: Response returned after updating one or more fields on a custom object. Confirms the object that was modified and the field values that were applied.

fields.description: Map of field names to their new values as applied during the update. Only the fields that were included in the update request are present.

id.description: ID of the custom object that was updated (`cobj_...`).

DeviceAuthorizationResponse.description: The initial response from an OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant request, containing the codes and URIs needed to complete device authentication.

device_code.description: Opaque code identifying this device authorization session. Pass this value when polling the token endpoint; do not display it to the user.

expires_in.description: Number of seconds until the `device_code` and `user_code` expire. After expiry the user must restart the authorization flow.

interval.description: Minimum number of seconds to wait between polling attempts on the token endpoint. Polling more frequently will result in a `slow_down` error.

user_code.description: Short alphanumeric code the user must enter at `verification_uri` to authorize the device.

verification_uri.description: URL the user visits to enter the `user_code` and approve the authorization request.

verification_uri_complete.description: Full verification URL with the `user_code` pre-filled as a query parameter. Display this as a QR code or deep link to reduce manual entry.

DeviceAuthorizationStatusResponse.description: The result of a completed OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization flow, indicating whether the user approved or denied the device's access request.

status.description: Outcome of the device authorization request. One of `"approved"` (the user granted access) or `"denied"` (the user rejected or cancelled the request).

HealthActionListResponse.description: List response containing agent health actions for a given agent or organization.

data.description: An example description.
data.title: Example Title

data.description: Array of agent health action objects representing setup checklist items and probe-detected issues.

ImageSource.description: Resolved metadata for an image, including its delivery URL, dimensions, and optional references to the underlying storage file or media record.

file.description: ID of the underlying storage file (`fil_...`). `null` when the image is not backed by a platform storage file.

height.description: Height of the image in pixels. `null` if not known.

media.description: ID of the associated media record (`med_...`). `null` when the image is not linked to a media entity.

mime_type.description: MIME type of the image, e.g. `"image/png"` or `"image/jpeg"`. `null` if not known.

refresh_url.description: Endpoint URL you can call to obtain a fresh signed `url` when the current one has expired. `null` if the URL does not require refreshing.

url.description: Signed or public URL for downloading the image. May be time-limited; use `refresh_url` to obtain a new URL when this one expires.

width.description: Width of the image in pixels. `null` if not known.

Installation.description: An installation representing a connection between an agent and an external service or enablement channel. Tracks configuration, lifecycle state, and any bound integration.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this installation (`agi_...`). `null` if the installation has no agent owner.

config.description: Kind-specific configuration object for this installation. Shape depends on the `kind` value. `null` if the kind requires no configuration.

kind.description: Slug identifying the type of external service this installation connects to, e.g. `"enablement/github_app"` or `"integration/gmail"`. `null` if not set.

lookup_key.description: Caller-assigned stable identifier for this installation, used to reference it in knowledge search `source_refs`. `null` if no lookup key was provided at creation time.

shared_integration.description: ID of the shared org- or app-level integration bound to this installation (`int_...`). `null` if no integration has been bound.

state.description: Current lifecycle state of the installation. One of `"pending"`, `"active"`, `"paused"`, or `"error"`. `"error"` indicates the installation was suspended due to a policy or compliance issue and requires attention.

status_payload.description: Provider-supplied status detail for this installation, set during activation or event processing. `null` if no status has been reported.

updated_at.description: When the installation record was last updated (ISO 8601).

InstallationKind.description: A supported installation kind describing a category of external service or enablement channel an agent can be connected to.

accepts_sources.description: When `true`, sources can be attached to installations of this kind to supply additional context to the agent.

category.description: Grouping category for UI display purposes, e.g. `"enablement"` or `"integration"`. `null` if uncategorized.

config_schema.description: JSON Schema object describing the shape of the `config` parameter accepted when creating or updating an installation of this kind. `null` if the kind accepts no configuration.

description.description: Short prose description of what this kind connects to and how it is used. `null` if no description is defined.

kind.description: Unique slug identifying this installation kind, e.g. `"enablement/github_app"`, `"integration/gmail"`, or `"web/site"`. Pass this value as `kind` when creating an installation.

label.description: Human-readable display name for this kind, e.g. `"GitHub App"`. `null` if the kind has no label defined.

provider.description: Identifier of the external provider this kind connects to, e.g. `"github"` or `"slack"`. `null` for kinds with no specific provider.

requires_integration.description: When `true`, this kind requires an integration to be provided (either inline or via `shared_integration`) before the installation can be activated.

InstallationKindListResponse.description: List response containing all available installation kinds that can be used when configuring an agent installation.

data.description: An example description.
data.label: GitHub App

data.description: Array of installation kind objects describing the available integration types and their configuration requirements.

InstallationListResponse.description: Paginated list response containing installation objects for an agent.

data.description: Array of installation objects returned for the current page.

InstallationSource.description: A source attached to an installation that supplies content for the agent's context. Sources are processed asynchronously after creation.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this source (`agi_...`). `null` if the source is not agent-owned.

context_installation.description: ID of the installation this source belongs to (`cin_...`). `null` if the source is not attached to an installation.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata associated with this source. Shape is caller-defined. `null` if no metadata was set.

parent_source.description: ID of the parent source (`cso_...`) when this source was derived from another source. `null` for top-level sources.

payload.description: Type-specific payload provided when the source was created. The shape depends on the `type` value. `null` if no payload was supplied.

state.description: Current lifecycle state of this source. One of `"active"` (ingestion running normally) or `"paused"` (ingestion suspended). Note that per-run ingestion progress is tracked separately and is not exposed on this field.

team.description: ID of the team associated with this source (`tem_...`). `null` if the source has no team association.

thread.description: ID of the conversation thread linked to this source (`thr_...`). `null` if the source is not thread-scoped.

type.description: Slug identifying the kind of content this source provides, e.g. `"file/document"` or `"web/link"`. `null` if the type is not set.

updated_at.description: When the source record was last updated (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user associated with this source (`usr_...`). `null` if the source has no user association.

InstallationSourceListResponse.description: Paginated list response containing installation source objects attached to an installation.

data.description: Array of installation source objects returned for the current page.

InstalledConfigEntry.description: A slim summary of a single config record created during an agent install transaction. Returned as an entry in `AgentCreateResponse.installed_configs`.

id.description: ID of the persisted config record (`cfg_...`).

key.description: Caller-supplied correlation key echoed back from the request. For top-level configs this is the original `lookup_key` (before any suffix is applied). For skill file children it is the composite `"<skill_lookup_key>:<relative_path>"` string, since file rows have no lookup_key of their own.

kind.description: Type of config that was created. One of `"Skill"`, `"File"`, `"Script"`, `"AgentTemplate"`, or `"Config"`.

lookup_key.description: Stored `lookup_key` for this config after any suffix has been applied. `null` for `File` children inside a skill bundle, which are keyed by `(parent_id, relative_path)` rather than by `lookup_key`.

InviteCreator.description:
A minimal, public-safe projection of the user who sent an invite, exposed to unauthenticated recipients so they can render a join screen.
Only identity fields are included; sensitive fields such as email address and organization membership are omitted.

name.description: Display name of the inviter. `null` when the inviter has not set a name on their account.

profile_picture.description: Profile picture of the inviter. `null` when the inviter has no profile picture set.

KeyValueStorageEntry.description: A single key-value storage entry belonging to a user. Represents one key/value pair written to a user's isolated storage namespace within an app.

created_at.description: When this storage entry was first created (ISO 8601). `null` if not yet persisted.

key.description: The string key used to store and look up this entry.

updated_at.description: When this storage entry was last updated (ISO 8601). `null` if not yet persisted.

user.description: ID of the user who owns this storage entry (`usr_...`).

value.description: The string value stored under `key` for this user.

KeyValueStorageEntryPage.description: Paginated response envelope for the dual-mode key-value storage list endpoint. End-user (user-JWT) callers receive only `data`; developer and server-to-server callers also receive pagination metadata fields.

data.description: Array of key-value storage entries for the current page.

has_next.description: Whether a subsequent page exists. `false` when the current page is the last page. Present only for developer and server-to-server callers.

has_prev.description: Whether a preceding page exists. `false` when the current page is the first page. Present only for developer and server-to-server callers.

page.description: Current page number (1-indexed). Present only for developer and server-to-server callers.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results returned per page. Present only for developer and server-to-server callers.

total_entries.description: Total number of storage entries matching the applied filters across all pages. Present only for developer and server-to-server callers.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages available given the current `page_size`. Present only for developer and server-to-server callers.

KeyValueStorageEntryWithUser.description: A key-value storage entry enriched with owner information. Developer and server-to-server callers receive `user_email` and `user_name` populated; end-user (user-JWT) callers receive those fields as `null`.

created_at.description: When this storage entry was first created (ISO 8601).

key.description: The string key used to store and look up this entry.

updated_at.description: When this storage entry was last updated (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user who owns this storage entry (`usr_...`).

user_email.description: Email address of the owning user. `null` for end-user (user-JWT) callers; populated for developer and server-to-server callers.

user_name.description: Display name of the owning user. `null` for end-user (user-JWT) callers; populated for developer and server-to-server callers.

value.description: The string value stored under `key` for this user.

KnowledgeSource.description: A knowledge source that ingests content into the knowledge base. Sources connect to external systems (e.g. Gmail, GitHub) and continuously or on-demand index items for search.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this source (`agt_...`). `null` if owned by a human user or team.

context_installation.description: ID of the context installation that provisioned this source (`cin_...`). `null` when the source was created directly rather than through an installation.

created_at.description: When this knowledge source was created (ISO 8601).

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this source. Useful for storing caller-defined labels or references.

org.description: ID of the organization this source belongs to (`org_...`). `null` if not scoped to an org.

parent_source.description: ID of the parent knowledge source (`cso_...`) when this source was derived from another. `null` for top-level sources.

payload.description: Type-specific configuration object. The keys depend on the source `type`; see the create endpoint for the expected shape per type.

sandbox.description: ID of the developer sandbox this source is scoped to (`sbx_...`). `null` outside sandbox contexts.

state.description: Current lifecycle state of the source. One of `"active"` (ingestion running normally) or `"paused"` (ingestion suspended).

team.description: ID of the team that owns this source (`tea_...`). `null` if owned by a user, agent, or org.

thread.description: ID of the chat thread this source is associated with (`thr_...`). `null` when not thread-scoped.

type.description: Source type identifier (e.g. `"gmail"`, `"github_activity"`). Determines the shape of `payload` and the ingestion behavior.

updated_at.description: When this knowledge source was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user that owns this source (`usr_...`). `null` if owned by a team, agent, or org.

KnowledgeSourceKind.description: Describes a single knowledge source kind that can be created through the public API. Use the `type` value when creating a new knowledge source.

description.description: Short description of what this source kind ingests and how it is used.

label.description: Human-readable display name for this source kind, suitable for showing in a UI.

type.description: Machine-readable type identifier for this source kind (e.g. `"gmail"`, `"github_activity"`). Pass this value as `type` when creating a knowledge source.

KnowledgeSourceKindListResponse.description: List response containing the knowledge source kinds available for creation via the API.

data.description: An example description.
data.label: Gmail

data.description: Array of knowledge source kind objects describing each creatable source type.

LLMConfig.description: LLM invocation settings for a routine or chain step. When present, overrides the agent-level model selection.

model.description: Model identifier to use for this routine or step, e.g. `"claude-sonnet-4-5"`. When omitted, the agent's default model is used.

MediaVariant.description: A processed variant of a media item, such as the original upload or a resized thumbnail, including a signed download URL resolved at request time.

content_type.description: MIME type of this variant's file (e.g., `"image/jpeg"`, `"video/mp4"`). `null` if the file is not loaded.

file.description: ID of the underlying storage file that backs this variant (`fil_...`).

filename.description: Original filename of the uploaded file for this variant. `null` if the file is not loaded.

height.description: Height of this variant in pixels. `null` if not recorded.

image_source.description: Resolved image delivery metadata for this variant, including dimensions and CDN URL. `null` for non-image content types.

updated_at.description: When this variant was last updated (ISO 8601).

url.description: Signed download URL for this variant, resolved at request time. `null` if the file is unavailable.

variant_key.description: Identifier for this variant's processing tier. Common values include `"original"` (the unmodified upload) and `"thumbnail"` (a resized preview).

width.description: Width of this variant in pixels. `null` if not recorded.

Message.description: A chat message posted in a thread, including its content, author, attachments, reactions, and optional reply metadata.

example.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

reply_to.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

actors.description: Resolved actor descriptors for the message sender, combining identity and display metadata. Always contains exactly one entry.

agent.description: ID of the agent user that sent this message (`agi_...`). `null` for messages sent by human users.

attachments.description: Files, links, tasks, media, artifacts, and actions attached to this message. Empty array if there are no attachments.

branched_thread.description: ID of the thread that was branched from this message (`thr_...`). `null` if this message has not spawned a branch thread.

content.description: Text content of the message. `null` for messages that contain only attachments.
content.example: Hello, how can I help you today?

has_replies.description: Whether this message has at least one reply. Only present when explicitly requested or computed by the server.

idempotency_key.description: Client-supplied idempotency key used to deduplicate message sends. `null` if the sender did not provide one.

legacy_agent.description: Identifier of the legacy chat agent that sent this message, if applicable. `null` for messages sent by users or modern agent users.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the message. Always present; defaults to an empty object when no metadata has been set.

org.description: ID of the organization that owns this message (`org_...`).

reactions.description: Emoji and other reactions added to this message by users. Empty array if no reactions have been added or the association is not preloaded.

rendering_mode.description: Display hint for how the message should be rendered. One of `"reply"`, `"direct"`, or `"inline"`. `null` for user-authored messages, which are always rendered as standard replies.

replies.description: Inline array of reply messages, each serialized as a full message object. Only present when the server has preloaded replies for this message.

replies_after_cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor to fetch replies posted after the current page. Only present when inline replies are included in the response.

replies_before_cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor to fetch replies posted before the current page. Only present when inline replies are included in the response.

reply_count.description: Total number of direct replies to this message. Only present when explicitly requested or computed by the server.

reply_to.description: The parent message this message is a reply to, expanded as a full message object when loaded. `null` if this is a top-level message or the association is not preloaded.

example.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

sandbox.description: ID of the developer sandbox this message belongs to (`dsb_...`). `null` for non-sandbox messages.

team.description: ID of the team this message is scoped to (`tem_...`). `null` if the message is not team-scoped.

thread.description: ID of the thread this message belongs to (`thr_...`). `null` for messages not yet associated with a thread.

user.description: The human user who sent this message. Returns a public ID string (`usr_...`) when the association is not preloaded, or an expanded user object when it is. `null` for messages sent by agents.

MessageReaction.description: A compact reaction record embedded in a message's `reactions` array, representing a single user's reaction to a message.

payload.description: Type-specific reaction data. For `"emoji_reaction"` reactions, contains an `emoji` key with the Unicode emoji string (e.g., `"👍"`).

type.description: Reaction type identifier. Currently always `"emoji_reaction"` for emoji-based reactions.

user.description: Public ID of the user who added the reaction (`usr_...`).

Notification.description: An inbox notification delivered to a recipient user. Includes type-specific render data resolved at request time.

archived_at.description: When the recipient archived this notification. `null` if the notification has not been archived.

read_at.description: When the recipient marked this notification read. `null` if the notification has not been read.

rendered.description: Type-specific render spec resolved at request time. All types include `title`, `kind`, and `actions`; custom types may add their own keys. Notifications whose type is no longer registered render with `kind: "unknown"`.

status.description: Current read state of the notification. One of `"unread"`, `"read"`, or `"archived"`.

type.description: Notification type slug, e.g. `"app_info"` for a built-in type or `"custom:deploy_complete"` for a custom type.

NotificationPreference.description: A single per-channel notification preference for the authenticated viewer, scoped to a notification type and optional app.

app_id.description: App this preference is scoped to (`app_...`). `null` indicates a system-level (no-app) slot that applies across all apps.

channel.description: Delivery channel for this preference, e.g. `"email"`. The `in_app` channel is always active and never has a preference row.

created_at.description: When this preference record was created (ISO 8601).

enabled.description: Whether delivery over this channel is enabled for the given type-and-app combination. `false` suppresses delivery even when the notification is triggered.

type.description: Notification type in wire-format. Built-in types use their atom name, e.g. `"app_info"` or `"billing_alert"`. Custom notification types use the form `"custom:<lookup_key>"`.

updated_at.description: When this preference record was last modified (ISO 8601).

NotificationPreferenceList.description: The complete set of notification preferences belonging to the authenticated viewer.

data.description: Array of notification preference objects for the authenticated viewer. Each entry corresponds to a distinct type-and-channel combination.

OAuthTokenResponse.description: A successful OAuth 2.0 token response. Issued by the token endpoint after a completed authorization or device-flow grant.

access_token.description: Bearer token used to authenticate API requests. Include this value in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.

expires_in.description: Number of seconds until the access token expires.

refresh_token.description: Token that can be exchanged for a new access token once the current one expires. `null` if the grant type does not issue refresh tokens.

scope.description: Space-separated list of scopes granted to the access token. `null` if scope was not included in the grant request.

user.description: The authenticated user associated with this token. `null` when the token is not tied to a specific user (e.g. client-credentials grants).

PaginatedReplies.description: A paginated list of reply messages for a thread. The reply array is returned directly, not nested inside a `data` wrapper.

replies.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

after_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as the pagination cursor to retrieve the page of replies that follow this one. `null` when no further pages exist.

before_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as the pagination cursor to retrieve the page of replies that precede this one. `null` when no earlier pages exist.

has_more.description: Whether additional reply pages exist beyond the current page.

replies.description: Array of reply message objects for the current page.

total_count.description: Total number of replies in the thread across all pages.

PresetConfig.description: Configuration for a preset routine handler. Controls the agent's behavior, session persistence, and model selection for a given routine or chain step.

example.instructions: You are a helpful assistant. Answer questions concisely and cite sources when possible.

instructions.description: Custom task or behavior instructions for the preset (max 10,000 chars).
instructions.example: You are a helpful assistant. Answer questions concisely and cite sources when possible.

llm.description: LLM invocation settings (e.g. a `model` override for this routine/step).

session_mode.description: Session mode: `stateless` (default, new session per trigger) or `session` (find-or-create a persistent session scoped by `session_scope`).

session_scope.description: When `session_mode` is `session`, controls session scoping: `per_user` (default), `per_key`, `per_org`, or `global`.

structured_message_template_ids.description: IDs of structured message templates that constrain the agent's responses to predefined structured formats.

RoutinePreset.description: A named preset that defines the execution model and constraints for a routine. Presets are shared definitions; individual routines reference a preset by name.

applicable_events.description: Event types that routines using this preset may be triggered by. `["*"]` means the preset accepts any event type. Routines assigned to this preset will be rejected at creation time if their trigger event is not in this list.

chainable.description: Whether routines using this preset can be composed as a step inside a chain routine. Presets with sessionable or asynchronous execution models are not chainable.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the preset does and when to use it.

label.description: Human-readable display name for the preset, suitable for use in UIs.

name.description: Stable machine identifier for the preset, e.g. `"do_task"`. Used when assigning a preset to a routine.

sessionable.description: Whether routines using this preset maintain a persistent conversation session across invocations. Sessionable presets do not expose instruction or session-mode configuration on individual routines.

unique.description: Whether at most one routine with this preset may exist per agent. Attempting to create a second routine with a unique preset on the same agent will be rejected.

SlackChannelBinding.description: A binding that connects a Slack channel to an ArchAstro team and one or more agents, enabling those agents to receive and respond to messages in that channel.

agents.description: IDs of the agents attached to this binding. Empty array when no agents are assigned.

channel.description: Slack channel ID (e.g. `C01234ABCDE`) that this binding targets.

customer_label.description: Human-readable label identifying the customer, derived from the binding's embedded config. `null` when not set.

id.description: Unique identifier for this Slack channel binding.

integration.description: ID of the Slack integration that owns this binding.

is_ext_shared_cached.description: Cached value of Slack's `is_ext_shared` flag for this channel. May be stale relative to Slack's current state.

team.description: ID of the ArchAstro team this channel is bound to.

SlackChannelBindingListResponse.description: Paginated list of Slack channel bindings for the requested integration or team. Use the `page` and `per_page` fields to navigate pages of results.

data.description: Array of Slack channel binding objects for the current page.

per_page.description: Maximum number of bindings returned per page.

total_count.description: Total number of Slack channel bindings matching the query across all pages.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages available at the current `per_page` size.

SolutionCategoryListResponse.description: Paginated list of solution category summaries. Use `page` and `page_size` to navigate pages of results.

data.description: An example description.
data.name: Example Name

data.description: Array of solution category summary objects for the current page.

has_next.description: `true` when a subsequent page of results exists.

has_prev.description: `true` when a previous page of results exists.

page_size.description: Maximum number of entries returned per page.

total_entries.description: Total number of distinct solution categories across all pages.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages available at the current `page_size`.

SolutionCategorySummary.description: A solution category that organizes solutions in the catalog, identified by a stable key and optionally nested under a parent category.

created_at.description: When this category was first created (ISO 8601). `null` for system-built-in categories.

description.description: Short prose description of what solutions in this category do. `null` when not configured.

key.description: Stable, human-readable key for this category, referenced by solutions via `category_keys`.

kind.description: Resource type identifier. Always `"SolutionCategory"`.

lookup_key.description: Lookup key of the underlying config record. `null` when not set.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this category by the publisher.

name.description: Display name shown to users. `null` when not configured.

org.description: Organization ID (`org_...`) that owns this category. `null` for system-scoped categories.

owners.description: Scopes under which this category is visible. Possible values are `"system"` (available to all apps) and `"org"` (scoped to the viewer's organization).

parent_key.description: Key of the parent `SolutionCategory`, enabling a hierarchy. `null` for top-level categories.

sort_order.description: Numeric hint for ordering categories in a list. Lower values sort first. `null` when not configured.

updated_at.description: When this category was last modified (ISO 8601). `null` for system-built-in categories.

virtual_path.description: Virtual path of the underlying config record. `null` when not set.

SolutionDependentAgent.description: A brief representation of an agent that references at least one config bundled by a Solution, included in the dependents preview response.

name.description: Human-readable display name of the agent. `null` when no name has been set.

SolutionDependentsResponse.description: A preview of the agents and configs that would be affected by deleting a Solution, returned before any deletion occurs so the caller can display a confirmation warning.

dependent_agent_count.description: Total number of distinct agents that reference at least one config bundled by this Solution. Use this count in the confirmation message; `dependent_agents` may be a shorter sample.

dependent_agents.description: A representative sample of the dependent agents, suitable for displaying in a warning list. May contain fewer entries than `dependent_agent_count` when there are many dependents.

preserved_config_count.description: Number of bundled configs that would be detached and preserved rather than deleted, because at least one live agent still references them.

SolutionDiffEntry.description: A single config entry in a solution upgrade diff, describing what action will be taken on a specific config key.

action.description: Planned action for this entry. One of `"add"` (new config), `"update"` (existing config changes), `"noop"` (no change needed), `"orphan"` (config no longer in the solution), or `"delete"` (config to be removed).

content_changed.description: `true` if the config content differs between the existing and incoming solution versions.

id.description: Config ID (`cfg_...`) if this entry corresponds to an existing config record. `null` for new additions.

key.description: Stable string key identifying this config entry within the solution.

kind.description: Config object type, e.g. `"Automation"` or `"Template"`. `null` if not yet known.

lookup_key.description: Human-readable stable identifier for this config. `null` if not assigned.

mime_type_changed.description: `true` if the MIME type of the config changed between versions.

referenced_by.description: List of other configs that reference this entry. Populated for orphaned configs that cannot be safely removed. Empty array when there are no references.

relative_path_changed.description: `true` if the relative path of the config within the solution changed between versions.

role.description: Role of this config within the solution. Indicates whether it is a primary config or a dependency.

virtual_path.description: Hierarchical path of this config in the config tree. `null` if not assigned.

SolutionDiffReference.description: A reference from another config to an orphaned entry in a solution upgrade diff, explaining why the orphan cannot be safely removed.

kind.description: Object type of the referencing config, e.g. `"Automation"` or `"Template"`.

lookup_key.description: Human-readable stable identifier of the referencing config. `null` if not assigned.

reason.description: Explanation of how the referencing config depends on the orphaned entry.

SolutionDiffSummary.description: Aggregate counts of each action type across all entries in a solution upgrade diff.

adds.description: Number of config entries that will be newly created by this upgrade.

deletes.description: Number of config entries that will be deleted as part of the upgrade.

noops.description: Number of config entries that are already up to date and require no changes.

orphans.description: Number of config entries present in the existing solution that are absent from the incoming version and have no external references blocking removal.

referenced_orphans.description: Number of orphaned config entries that cannot be removed because other configs still reference them.

updates.description: Number of config entries that exist and will be updated with new content.

SolutionImportResponse.description: The result of importing a Solution bundle into the library, including the Solution config record, a structured import result, and the list of all configs persisted during the transaction.

created_at.description: When the Solution config record was first created (ISO 8601).

import_result.description: Structured outcome of the import, including status, conflict details, and version information.

installed_configs.description: Deprecated legacy field. One entry per persisted config in the import (including the Solution itself), defaulting to an empty array. Callers should prefer `solution` plus follow-up APIs instead. `key` echoes the caller-supplied input identifier (original lookup_key for top-level configs; `<skill_lookup_key>:<relative_path>` for skill / solution-file children). Order is stable: sorted by `key`.

kind.description: Resource type. Always `"Solution"`.

lookup_key.description: The `lookup_key` stored on the Solution config after the import's suffix normalization. `null` when the Solution was not given a lookup key.

solution.description: Full summary of the imported Solution, in the same shape as the individual Solution retrieval endpoint.

updated_at.description: When the Solution config record was last modified (ISO 8601).

virtual_path.description: The `virtual_path` stored on the Solution config, used as the stable dedupe key across owner scopes. `null` when no virtual path was assigned.

SolutionImportResult.description: The machine-readable outcome of a Solution import attempt, indicating whether the import succeeded or requires an upgrade flow to resolve a version conflict.

code.description: Machine-readable conflict code present when `status` is `"conflict"`, identifying the specific conflict reason. `null` when `status` is `"ready"`.

dry_run.description: Whether this result was produced by a dry-run check. `true` when the import was validated without persisting any changes.

existing_solution_version.description: Semver string of the Solution version already present in the library. `null` when no prior version exists.

incoming_solution_version.description: Semver string of the Solution version in the bundle being imported. `null` when the bundle does not declare a version.

message.description: Human-readable description of the import status or conflict reason, suitable for display in a confirmation dialog. `null` when no detail is available.

status.description: Outcome of the import check. `"ready"` means the import can proceed as a normal create or update. `"conflict"` means a version conflict was detected and the upgrade flow must be used instead.

upgrade_required.description: Whether the caller must invoke the dedicated upgrade flow to complete the import. Mirrors `status == "conflict"` as a convenience boolean.

SolutionInstallResponse.description: The runtime resource provisioned by installing a Solution, along with a reference back to the source Solution config.

id.description: Public ID of the provisioned resource. The prefix reflects the resource kind: `agi_...` for Agent, `aut_...` for Automation, `art_...` for AgentRoutine, `att_...` for AgentTool, `ask_...` for AgentSkill, `cmp_...` for AgentComputer.

kind.description: Type of the provisioned resource. One of `"Agent"`, `"Automation"`, `"AgentRoutine"`, `"AgentTool"`, `"AgentSkill"`, or `"AgentComputer"`.

lookup_key.description: The `lookup_key` stamped on the provisioned resource. `null` for `AgentSkill`, which is a join record and does not carry a lookup key.

solution.description: Solution config ID (`cfg_...`) that was used as the source for this install.

SolutionListResponse.description: A paginated collection of Solution summaries, with page metadata for navigating the result set.

data.description: An example description.
data.name: Example Name

data.description: Array of Solution summary objects for the current page, in the order returned by the query.

has_next.description: `true` when a subsequent page exists; `false` when this is the last page.

has_prev.description: `true` when a preceding page exists; `false` when this is the first page.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results included per page.

total_entries.description: Total number of Solutions matching the query after deduplication by `solution_id` across owner scopes.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages available at the current `page_size`.

SolutionSummary.description: A catalog entry for an imported Solution, including its display metadata, bundled templates, owner scopes, and any available upgrade information.

category_keys.description: Category tag keys declared in the Solution body, used to group Solutions in the catalog. An empty array when the body declares none.

created_at.description: When the Solution config was first imported (ISO 8601).

description.description: Short tagline or summary declared in the Solution body, used as the card subhead in catalog UIs. `null` when the Solution body does not set one.

kind.description: Resource type. Always `"Solution"`.

latest_solution.description: When `upgrade_available` is `true`, the system-scope Solution config ID (`cfg_...`) that should be used as the upgrade source. `null` otherwise.

latest_version.description: When `upgrade_available` is `true`, the higher system-scope `solution_version` available to upgrade to. `null` otherwise.

lookup_key.description: The lookup key stored on the Solution config, if one was assigned during import. `null` when no lookup key was set.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata declared in the Solution body (e.g. category or display hints). Present as an empty object when the body declares none.

name.description: Human-facing display name declared in the Solution body. `null` when the Solution body does not set one.

org.description: Organization ID (`org_...`) that owns this Solution config, when the Solution is scoped to a specific org. `null` for system-scope (app-level) Solutions.

org_logo.description: Canonical image-source object for the resolved `org`'s logo, used as the principal category section glyph. Carries the signed `url` plus a `refresh_url`. `null` when `org_slug` is `null` or the org has no logo.

org_name.description: Display name of the resolved `org`. Pairs with `org_slug` as the principal catalog category's label. `null` when `org_slug` is `null`.

org_slug.description: Resolved slug of the Solution body's `org` (the publishing organization), when set and it resolves to a real org visible to the viewer. When present this is the Solution's principal catalog category key — clients group the Solution under this org ahead of `category_keys`. `null` when the body has no `org` or it doesn't resolve.

owners.description: Owner scopes this Solution appears under. Members: `"system"` (app-level system scope) and/or `"org"` (viewer's org scope).

readme_url.description: Relative path to the public README endpoint with a signed token already embedded. `null` when the Solution has no README. Token expires in 1 hour — refresh via `GET /api/v1/solutions/:solution`.

solution_id.description: Stable UUID declared in the Solution body, used to identify the same logical Solution across multiple installed copies and owner scopes. `null` when the body omits it.

solution_version.description: Semver string declared in the Solution body (e.g. `"1.2.0"`). `null` when the body does not declare a version.

tag_keys.description: Freeform tag keys declared in the Solution body. An empty array when the body declares none.

template_kind.description: Wrapped template kind — `"AgentTemplate"`, `"AutomationTemplate"`, `"AgentRoutineTemplate"`, `"AgentToolTemplate"`, `"AgentComputerTemplate"`, or `"SolutionTemplateRef"` for ref-mode bundles.

templates.description: Template configs bundled by this Solution, in declaration order — the first entry is the deployable template the Solution wraps; the rest are sibling templates the wrapped template references.

updated_at.description: When the Solution config was last modified (ISO 8601).

upgrade_available.description: `true` when this Solution is installed at the viewer's org scope and the app-level system scope carries a higher `solution_version`. Always `false` for system-only rows.

virtual_path.description: The stable virtual path assigned to this Solution config, used as the deduplication key when the same Solution appears under multiple owner scopes. `null` when unset.

SolutionTagListResponse.description: Paginated list of solution tag summaries returned by the list solution tags endpoint.

data.description: An example description.
data.name: Example Name

data.description: Array of solution tag objects for the current page.

has_next.description: `true` if a subsequent page exists; `false` when this is the last page.

has_prev.description: `true` if a preceding page exists; `false` when this is the first page.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results returned per page.

total_entries.description: Total number of distinct solution tags across all pages, after deduplication by key.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages available at the current `page_size`.

SolutionTagSummary.description: A single solution tag definition, representing a named classification label that can be applied to solutions.

created_at.description: When the solution tag was first created (ISO 8601). `null` if unavailable.

description.description: Short prose explanation of what the tag represents. `null` if not provided.

key.description: Stable string key for this tag, referenced by `Solution.tag_keys` to associate solutions with this tag.

kind.description: Object type discriminator. Always `"SolutionTag"`.

lookup_key.description: Human-readable stable identifier for this tag config, used for lookups and imports. `null` if not assigned.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this tag. Empty object `{}` when no metadata is present.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the tag. `null` if not yet set.

org.description: ID of the organization that owns this tag (`org_...`). `null` for system-scoped tags.

owners.description: Scopes under which this tag is visible to the caller. One or both of `"system"` (platform-level tag available to all orgs) and `"org"` (tag scoped to the viewer's org).

sort_order.description: Optional integer hint for ordering tags in UI lists. Lower values sort first. `null` if not set.

updated_at.description: When the solution tag was last modified (ISO 8601). `null` if unavailable.

virtual_path.description: Hierarchical path used to organize this tag in the config tree. `null` if not assigned.

SolutionTemplateSummary.description: Identity and display metadata for a single template bundled by a Solution, used to represent each wrapped or sibling template at template granularity.

description.description: Short prose blurb from the template body's `description:` field. `null` when the body doesn't set one. Used as the card subhead in the Library carousel.

display_name.description: Human-facing label from the template body's `display_name:` field. `null` when the body doesn't set one. Library carousels use this for the card title, falling back to a humanized `name`.

id.description: Template config ID (`cfg_...`). `null` for inline-only templates.

kind.description: Template config kind, or `SolutionTemplateRef` / `SolutionTemplatePath` when unresolved.

lookup_key.description: Lookup key stamped on the template config at import time. `null` when no lookup key was assigned.

name.description: Canonical name from the template body. For `AgentTemplate` this doubles as the human-facing label; for `AgentToolTemplate` it's the LLM-facing tool function identifier (snake_case); for `AgentRoutineTemplate` it's the routine identifier (kebab-case). Clients rendering carousels should prefer `display_name` and fall back to humanizing `name`.

readme_url.description: Relative path to the public README endpoint with a signed token already embedded, scoped to this template's bundled markdown asset. `null` when the Solution body's `templates[].readme_path` is unset for this entry. Token expires in 1 hour — refresh via `GET /api/v1/solutions/:solution`.

virtual_path.description: Stable virtual path assigned to the template config. `null` when no virtual path was set.

SolutionUpgradeResponse.description: Response returned by the solution upgrade endpoint, containing the solution record, the full upgrade diff, and the resulting installed configs.

created_at.description: When the solution config record was first created (ISO 8601). `null` if unavailable.

id.description: Config ID of the solution record (`cfg_...`).

installed_configs.description: List of config entries that were installed or updated as part of this upgrade. Empty when `dry_run` is `true` or when no configs changed.

kind.description: Object type discriminator. Always `"Solution"`.

lookup_key.description: Human-readable stable identifier for this solution config. `null` if not assigned.

solution.description: Summary of the solution being upgraded, including its name, manifest metadata, and tag keys.

updated_at.description: When the solution config record was last modified (ISO 8601). `null` if unavailable.

upgrade_result.description: Detailed result of the upgrade operation, including the computed diff and any conflict information.

virtual_path.description: Hierarchical path of the solution in the config tree. `null` if not assigned.

SolutionUpgradeResult.description: The outcome of a solution upgrade operation, including the computed diff and conflict status.

changes.description: Ordered list of individual config change entries representing every add, update, noop, orphan, and delete in the diff.

code.description: Machine-readable conflict code when `status` is `"conflict"`, e.g. `"review_required"`. `null` when there is no conflict.

dry_run.description: `true` when the upgrade was computed without writing any changes; `false` when changes were committed.

existing_solution_version.description: Version string of the currently installed solution, as declared in its manifest. `null` if no prior version is installed.

incoming_solution_version.description: Version string of the incoming solution to be installed, as declared in its manifest. `null` if the incoming manifest omits a version.

message.description: Human-readable description of the conflict or error. `null` when there is no conflict.

review_fingerprint.description: Opaque fingerprint that uniquely identifies this diff. Pass this value as `review_fingerprint` on a subsequent non-dry-run upgrade call to confirm you have reviewed the diff. `null` if not applicable.

status.description: Overall result of the upgrade. `"ready"` means the upgrade can proceed; `"conflict"` means a blocking issue was detected and the upgrade was not applied.

summary.description: Aggregate counts of each action type across all diff entries.

version_change.description: Describes the nature of the version transition. One of `"upgrade"`, `"downgrade"`, `"same"`, or `"unknown"`.

StorageFile.description: A file stored in the platform's object storage, with metadata and a signed URL for downloading its contents.

content_type.description: MIME type of the file, e.g. `"image/png"` or `"application/pdf"`.

filename.description: Original filename as provided at upload time.

image_source.description: Image display metadata. Present only when `content_type` is an image type; `null` otherwise.

org.description: ID of the organization that owns this file (`org_...`).

sandbox.description: ID of the sandbox this file is scoped to (`sbx_...`). `null` for files not associated with a sandbox.

size.description: Size of the file in bytes.

updated_at.description: When the file record was last modified (ISO 8601).

url.description: Short-lived signed URL for downloading the file. `null` if a URL could not be generated.

Team.description: A team within an organization, used to group users and agents and scope resources like configs, agents, and tasks.

acl.description: Access control list governing visibility and join permissions for this team. `null` when no ACL restrictions are applied and the team inherits default access rules.

app.description: ID of the developer application this team belongs to (`dap_...`). `null` if the team is not scoped to an app.

badges.description: Aggregated badge counts for the team, keyed by category. `null` when badge data is not loaded.

description.description: Human-readable description of the team's purpose. `null` if not set.

membership_status.description: The authenticated viewer's role on this team. One of `"owner"`, `"admin"`, or `"member"`. `null` if the viewer is not a member.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this team. Returns an empty object when no metadata has been set.

name.description: Display name of the team.

org.description: ID of the organization this team belongs to (`org_...`). `null` if the team is not org-scoped.

sandbox.description: ID of the developer sandbox this team is scoped to (`dsb_...`). `null` outside sandbox contexts.

slug.description: URL-safe slug for the team, derived from the team name. `null` if not set.

updated_at.description: When this team was last updated (ISO 8601).

TeamInvite.description: A team invite containing a short alphanumeric code that other users can present to join the team.

code.description: Short alphanumeric join code. Pass this value as `join_code` to the join-with-code endpoint to add a user to the team.

TeamMembership.description: A record representing a user's or agent's membership in a team, including their resolved identity details and role.

agent.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
agent.name: Example Name

agent.description: The agent associated with this membership, as an expanded agent object. `null` when the member is a user, the type is unknown, or the association is not preloaded.

created_at.description: When this membership record was created (ISO 8601).

joined_at.description: When the principal joined the team (ISO 8601).

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to this membership record. `null` if no metadata has been set.

name.description: Display name of the member, derived from the associated user or agent. `null` if the principal is unknown.

profile_picture.description: Profile picture of the member, derived from the associated user or agent. `null` if not set or principal is unknown.

role.description: The member's role within the team. One of `"owner"`, `"admin"`, or `"member"`.

team.description: The team this membership belongs to, as an expanded team object. `null` when the team association is not preloaded.

type.description: Resolved principal type. One of `"user"`, `"agent"`, or `"unknown"` when the principal cannot be determined.

updated_at.description: When this membership record was last updated (ISO 8601).

user.description: The user associated with this membership, as an expanded user object. `null` when the member is an agent, the type is unknown, or the association is not preloaded.

TeamMembershipListResponse.description: A paginated list of team memberships returned by the list team memberships endpoint.

agent.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
agent.name: Example Name

data.description: Array of team membership objects for the current page.

has_next.description: `true` if a subsequent page exists; `false` when this is the last page.

has_prev.description: `true` if a previous page exists; `false` when this is the first page.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results returned per page.

total_entries.description: Total number of team memberships matching the applied filters across all pages.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages given the current `page_size`.

Thread.description: A chat thread, representing a conversation channel that can be owned by a user, team, or agent and may contain messages, participants, and AI agent activity.

parent_message.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

participating_agents.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
participating_agents.name: Example Name

agent_user.description: ID of the agent that owns this thread (`agt_...`). `null` for user-owned or team-owned threads.

creator.description: Expanded user object for the user who created this thread. Populated only when the association is loaded.

description.description: Optional description or purpose statement for the thread. `null` if not set.

is_channel.description: Whether this thread operates as a channel — a multi-member broadcast-style conversation.

is_default.description: Whether this is the default thread for its owner. Each user or team has at most one default thread.

is_transient.description: Whether this thread is ephemeral and may be deleted automatically after a period of inactivity or when its TTL expires.

is_unlisted.description: Whether this thread is hidden from public discovery. Unlisted threads are accessible only to direct participants.

key.description: Application-defined stable key that uniquely identifies the thread within its scope. Useful for idempotent creation. `null` if not set.

last_activity.description: When the last message or activity occurred in this thread. Present only when activity enrichment is requested.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the thread. Shape is application-defined; `null` if no metadata has been set.

muted.description: Whether the authenticated user has muted notifications for this thread. `true` suppresses all notification delivery.

org.description: ID of the organization this thread belongs to (`org_...`). `null` for threads outside an org context.

parent_message.description: The message that spawned this thread as a sub-thread. `null` for top-level threads.

participant.description: Array of participant user IDs (`usr_...`) who are members of this thread.

participants.description: Expanded participant user objects for each member of this thread. Populated only when the association is loaded.

participating_actor.description: Composite actor identifiers for all participants currently active in this thread. Present only when actor enrichment is requested.

participating_agents.description: Expanded agent objects for all agents participating in this thread. Present only when agent enrichment is requested.

role.description: The authenticated user's membership role in this thread, e.g. `"owner"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"`. `null` if the user is not a member.

sandbox.description: ID of the developer sandbox this thread is scoped to (`sbx_...`). `null` for production threads.

settings.description: Per-thread configuration settings controlling AI agent behavior for this thread.

slug.description: URL-safe slug for the thread, used in human-readable permalinks. `null` if not assigned.

sub_threads.description: Threads that are nested under this thread as replies to a parent message. Present only when sub-thread enrichment is requested.

team.description: ID of the team that owns this thread (`team_...`). `null` for user-owned or agent-owned threads.

title.description: Human-readable name of the thread. `null` if no title has been set.

ttl.description: Time-to-live in seconds after which the thread may be automatically cleaned up. `null` if the thread does not expire.

unread_count.description: Number of messages in this thread that the authenticated user has not yet read. Present only when read-state enrichment is requested.

updated_at.description: When the thread was last modified (ISO 8601).

user.description: ID of the user who owns this thread (`usr_...`). `null` for team-owned or agent-owned threads.

ThreadMember.description: A user's membership record in a thread, capturing their role and full user profile.

membership_type.description: Role of the member within the thread. One of `"owner"` (the user who created or was granted ownership) or `"member"` (a regular participant).

thread.description: ID of the thread this membership belongs to (`thr_...`).

user.description: Full user object for the member (`usr_...`). Present when the association is preloaded; `null` otherwise.

ThreadReadStatus.description: The read status of a thread for a specific user, indicating how far they have read and how many messages remain unread.

last_read_message.description: Message ID (`msg_...`) of the last message the user has read in this thread. `null` if the user has never read any message in the thread.

thread.description: Thread ID (`thr_...`) that this read status belongs to.

unread_count.description: Number of messages in the thread that the user has not yet read.

ThreadSettings.description: Configuration settings for a thread that control AI agent behavior and other thread-level preferences.

agent_enabled.description: Whether the AI agent is active for this thread. `true` enables AI responses; `false` disables them. Defaults to `true` when settings have not been explicitly configured.

UpgradeTemplateSummary.description: Compact summary of an AgentTemplate config referenced by an agent upgrade or source-solution response.

created_at.description: When this template config was created (ISO 8601).

description.description: Description of the template from the config body. `null` if the current version has no `description` field.

display_name.description: Human-readable display name from the config body. `null` if the current version has no `display_name` field.

kind.description: Config kind identifier for this template (e.g. `"agent_tool_template"`).

lookup_key.description: Stable lookup key assigned to this template config. `null` if no lookup key is set.

name.description: Template name as stored in the config body. `null` if the current version has no `name` field.

updated_at.description: When this template config was last modified (ISO 8601).

virtual_path.description: Virtual filesystem path for this template config. `null` if not set.

User.description: A platform user account. Represents a human or system actor that can own threads, belong to an organization, and interact with the API.

alias.description: Short handle or alias for the user. `null` if not set.

app.description: ID of the app this user (and their access token) is scoped to (`dap_...`). `null` if the user is not scoped to an app.

app_name.description: Display name of the user's app. `null` when the app association was not preloaded by the caller.

email.description: Email address of the user.

is_system_user.description: `true` if this account is an internal system user rather than a human. System users are created automatically by the platform.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the user. Defaults to an empty object.

name.description: Full display name of the user. `null` if the user has not set a name.

org.description: ID of the organization this user belongs to (`org_...`). `null` if the user is not a member of any organization.

org_name.description: Display name of the user's organization. `null` when the user is not in an org, or when the org association was not preloaded by the caller.

org_role.description: Role of the user within their organization. One of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"`. `null` when the user is not a member of any organization.

sandbox.description: ID of the sandbox environment this user is scoped to (`sbx_...`). `null` for production users.

sandbox_name.description: Display name of the user's sandbox environment. `null` for production users, or when the sandbox association was not preloaded by the caller.

UserInvite.description: A shareable invite created by a user, optionally scoped to a thread. Recipients can use the invite key to join or start a conversation.

key.description: Secret bearer token used to accept this invite. Treat this value like a password — do not log or expose it publicly.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to the invite at creation time. Defaults to an empty object.

thread.description: ID of the thread this invite is scoped to (`thr_...`). `null` if the invite is not bound to a thread.

user.description: The user who created this invite.

ValidationResult.description: The result of a configuration validation check, indicating whether the config is valid and listing any errors or warnings.

errors.description: List of human-readable error messages describing why validation failed. Empty or absent when `valid` is `true`.

valid.description: `true` if the configuration passed all validation checks, `false` if one or more errors were found.

warnings.description: List of human-readable warning messages emitted during validation. Warnings do not cause `valid` to be `false` but indicate potentially problematic configuration.

WorkerStatus.description: Execution state of the background worker processing a routine run. Reflects the current job status and retry progress.

attempt.description: Number of times the worker has been attempted so far. `0` means the job has been enqueued but not yet started.

max_attempts.description: Maximum number of attempts the worker is allowed before the job is marked `"discarded"`.

status.description: Current execution state of the worker. One of `"queued"`, `"executing"`, `"retrying"`, `"completed"`, `"discarded"`, or `"cancelled"`.

WorkingMemoryEntry.description: A key-value memory record stored for an agent, optionally scoped to a user. Memory entries persist across invocations and may carry an expiration time.

agent.description: ID of the agent that owns this memory entry (`agt_...`).

created_at.description: When this memory entry was first written (ISO 8601).

expires_at.description: When this entry will be automatically deleted. `null` if the entry does not expire.

key.description: The string key used to look up this memory entry within the agent's memory namespace.

updated_at.description: When this memory entry was last modified (ISO 8601).

value.description: The string value stored under `key`. May be any serialized content the agent wrote.

WorkingMemoryEntryListResponse.description: Paginated list of working memory entries stored for an agent. Includes page metadata to support sequential page traversal.

data.description: Array of working memory entry objects for the current page.

has_next.description: `true` if a subsequent page exists and can be fetched by incrementing the page number.

has_prev.description: `true` if a previous page exists and can be fetched by decrementing the page number.

page.description: The current page number, starting at `1`.

page_size.description: Maximum number of entries returned per page.

total_entries.description: Total number of working memory entries matching the query across all pages.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages given the current `page_size`.

info.description: Agent-first API for runtime + developer control-plane operations (users, teams, agents, routines, context, workflows, integrations, and webhooks).
info.title: ArchAstro Platform API

get.description:
Returns a cursor-paginated list of activity feed entries visible to the
authenticated user. Entries are ordered from newest to oldest by default.
Use `before_cursor` to page backward and `after_cursor` to page forward.

Results are scoped to the caller's app. All filter params are optional and
can be combined. Passing multiple values in an array filter returns entries
matching any of the supplied values (OR semantics).

Invalid `kind` or `level` values return a 400 error listing the accepted
values rather than being silently ignored.

parameters.description: One or more entry kinds to include. Accepted values: routine_run, automation_run, thread_story, agent_quality_verdict, generic. Omit to return all kinds.
parameters.name: kind

parameters.description: One or more severity levels to include. Accepted values: debug, info, warn, error, audit. Omit to return all levels.
parameters.name: level

parameters.description: Restrict results to entries associated with these agent IDs (`agt_...`). Accepts multiple values.
parameters.name: agent

parameters.description: Restrict results to entries associated with these thread IDs (`thr_...`). Accepts multiple values.
parameters.name: thread

parameters.description: Restrict results to entries associated with these team IDs (`tem_...`). Accepts multiple values.
parameters.name: team

parameters.description: Restrict results to entries associated with these organization IDs (`org_...`). Accepts multiple values.
parameters.name: org

parameters.description: Restrict results to entries that share this correlation group identifier. Useful for tracing a chain of related events.
parameters.name: correlation_id

parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return per page. Defaults to 50; maximum is 100.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `before_cursor` field. Returns entries older than the cursor's position.
parameters.name: before_cursor

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `after_cursor` field. Returns entries newer than the cursor's position.
parameters.name: after_cursor

schema.description: Cursor-paginated list of activity feed entries.

data.content: The agent completed the task successfully.
data.title: Example Title

after_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as `after_cursor` to retrieve the next newer page. Absent when no newer entries exist.

before_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as `before_cursor` to retrieve the next older page. Absent when no older entries exist.

data.description: Array of activity feed entry objects for the current page, ordered newest first.

example.content: The agent completed the task successfully.
example.title: Example Title

items.description: A single event record in an activity feed, capturing what happened, who caused it, and which resources were involved.

example.content: The agent completed the task successfully.
example.title: Example Title

agent.description: The agent that produced this event. Returns an agent ID (`agi_...`) by default, or an expanded agent object when the association is loaded. `null` if no agent is associated.

oneOf.description: An AI agent that can be configured with tools, routines, and skills, and invoked to handle conversations or tasks.

example.identity: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about ArchAstro products.
example.name: Example Name

acl.description: Access control list for the agent. Contains a `grants` array where each entry specifies `principal_type`, `principal`, and `actions`. `null` when no ACL restrictions are applied and the agent is accessible to all members of its scope.

add.description: Patch mode: grants to add or merge into the existing list. Cannot be combined with `grants`.

items.description: A single access-control grant that pairs a principal with the set of actions it is allowed to perform.

actions.description: Array of action strings the principal is permitted to perform, e.g. `["read", "write"]`. Must contain at least one entry.

principal.description: The identifier of the principal. A string ID for `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, and `"agent"` types; one of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"` for `"org_role"`; omit entirely when `principal_type` is `"everyone"`.

principal_type.description: The kind of principal receiving the grant. One of `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, `"org_role"`, `"agent"`, or `"everyone"`.

grants.description: Replace mode: the complete new list of grants that replaces all existing entries. Send an empty array (`[]`) to clear all grants. Cannot be combined with `add` or `remove`.

items.description: A single access-control grant that pairs a principal with the set of actions it is allowed to perform.

actions.description: Array of action strings the principal is permitted to perform, e.g. `["read", "write"]`. Must contain at least one entry.

principal.description: The identifier of the principal. A string ID for `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, and `"agent"` types; one of `"admin"`, `"member"`, or `"viewer"` for `"org_role"`; omit entirely when `principal_type` is `"everyone"`.

principal_type.description: The kind of principal receiving the grant. One of `"user"`, `"team"`, `"org"`, `"org_role"`, `"agent"`, or `"everyone"`.

remove.description: Patch mode: principals whose grants should be removed from the existing list. Cannot be combined with `grants`.

items.description: Identifies a principal to be removed from an access-control list.
